Plus, how Tadej Pogačar, Mathieu van der Poel, Wout van Aert, and Mads Pedersen all arrive heading into Sunday.

item.title: Carlton Kirby on French Biscuit Factories and Emotional Crescendos
item.description: Carlton Kirby chats with Matt Stephens about their first encounter in the Eurosport commentary booth more than 10 years ago. His love of cycling may have been born in a French biscuit factory, but it was the years after that summer job that saw him DJ in Tuvalu, become a breakfast broadcaster, and have an unfortunate encounter with the world’s biggest dog. If you think Matt is tangential, just wait until you hear some of Carlton’s stories! Warm, funny, and full of behind-the-scenes detail, this episode offers a unique look at the voices behind the racing and the stories that keep fans coming back for more. www.sigmasports.com Learn more about your ad choices. Get an update from Matson in Bruges and Flanders: E3 hangover, Gent-Wevelgem, dead legs in Dwars, and the dream of diamonds in Flanders. Featuring Anders Mielke and Mathias Sunekær Norsgaard. item.title: Annemiek van Vleuten - From €100 a Month to Tour de France Winner
item.description: One of the most decorated riders in modern cycling joins us on The Hotseat. Annemiek van Vleuten talks about her unconventional path to the top of the sport, from studying animal science and zoonotic diseases in Wageningen to becoming a Tour de France winner and World Champion. She began racing at 26 and explains how raw talent, relentless goal-setting, and a fearless mindset helped her rise through the peloton at a time when women’s cycling was still largely amateur. She reflects on the sport’s evolution, from earning just €100 a month to seeing the rapid growth, professionalism, and global attention the women’s peloton enjoys today. From fighting for position on the cobbles of the Tour of Flanders to handling the pressure and isolation of Grand Tour leadership, Van Vleuten offers a rare and honest look at what it really takes to win at the highest level and why, despite her dominance, she chose to walk away at her peak.

item.title: A Preview of the Tour of Flanders and Paris-Roubaix in Rolf’s Company
item.description: Anders Mielke has assembled a strong lineup for this episode of Radio Tour. To cover all the Danish victories and look ahead to the Tour of Flanders and Paris-Roubaix, he is joined by Matti Breschel, Anders Lund, and Rolf Sørensen.

item.title: 154. Harry Tanfield: Baptisms of Fire, Keeping the Faith, and the Ribble Outliers
item.description: This week James and Will are joined by Harry Tanfield. Born and raised in Yorkshire, Harry started racing at 10, and after catching teams’ attention with strong rides at races like Junior Paris-Roubaix, he turned professional at 20 with JLT Condor. ProConti racing became WorldTour after a standout 2018 season that included silver at the Commonwealth Games time trial, a British national track title, World Cup track wins, and an important stage win at the Tour de Yorkshire, making him the first Yorkshireman to win a stage of the race. The following year Harry joined WorldTour team Katusha-Alpecin on a two-year contract, but by the end of the season the team had folded. A few unsettled seasons followed, with moves to AG2R in 2020 and Qhubeka for 2021, but by 2022 Harry was back racing ProConti with Ribble-Weldtite, TDT, and then Saint Piran. Then, in 2025, he rejoined Ribble as part of the Ribble Outliers gravel team, for whom he still rides. In this conversation, Harry talks about the good old days of elbows-out crit racing, reflects on the apparent collapse of the British race calendar, speaks candidly about his turbulent time in the WorldTour and how he handled team collapse and personal tragedy, and discusses what the gravel scene offers riders coming from the road. Our chat begins at 6:19. This episode is brought to you by the Hammerhead Karoo GPS bike computer. item.title: The Monuments: Liège-Bastogne-Liège
item.description: La Doyenne. The old lady. Liège-Bastogne-Liège is older than any of the other Monuments. In this episode of Bagerstop’s Monument series, you can get close to the race in the beautiful, and treacherous, Ardennes in Wallonia, Belgium. Jakob Fuglsang, a former winner of the race, Brian Holm, and Bastian Emil Goldschmidt are ready to tell you about this brutal Monument here on Bagerstop. Support the show.

item.title: #30 Bak Lane: Het Laatste Nieuws with Valgren
item.description: Michael Valgren joins Bak Lane for a connection to Flanders. Hear an update from the Thybo native after Italian gravel, a Monument, and a stage win, as well as the Flemish cobbles and the outlook for the Tour of Flanders on Sunday. Host: Lars Bak. Bak Lane is produced by Anders Mielke / Forhjulslir.

item.title: Amazing Racing Through Flanders Fields
item.description: This week we review three major races and look ahead to Dwars Door Vlaanderen. At the E3 Saxo Classic, Mathieu van der Poel completed a hat trick, but only just, surviving a desperate final kilometre as his chasers hesitated at the crucial moment. Does that raise questions about his form for the Tour of Flanders? At Gent-Wevelgem, Van Aert and Van der Poel went clear together on the Kemmelberg in a rare head-to-head duel before being caught by the peloton near the finish. Jasper Philipsen took the win. At the Volta a Catalunya, Jonas Vingegaard was dominant from the first mountain stage onward, winning on the Coll de Pal and the Santuari de Queralt to secure the overall classification ahead of Evenepoel and Lipowitz. And with Dwars Door Vlaanderen on Wednesday, we look at the key favourites and five riders who could surprise. This episode is fuelled by MNSTRY. item.title: S1 Ep690: Is This the Death of Road Cycling - and the Future for Gravel?
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we discuss the controversial reality that cash-strapped councils are considering turning deteriorating paved roads back into gravel, and what that could mean for the future of road bikes. item.description: This week on the pod, G and Luke go through some of the major transfers for the upcoming season — and as always in cycling, there’s a lot of change to look forward to. Remco steals the headlines... item.title: Are Van der Poel and Van Aert Ready for Pogačar at Flanders? | Gent-Wevelgem & Catalunya Breakdown
item.description: Spencer Martin, George Hincapie, and Johan Bruyneel break down Jasper Philipsen’s win at Gent-Wevelgem, the battle between Wout van Aert and Mathieu van der Poel, and what it may mean for the Tour of Flanders, as well as Jonas Vingegaard’s dominant victory at the Volta a Catalunya. They discuss the key moments from both races, how chasing pelotons are getting better at bringing back dangerous moves, and, most importantly, whether anyone can stop Pogačar in the upcoming races. item.title: Vingegaard’s Catalonia and the Flemish Classic Week
item.description: Forhjulslir is presented in collaboration with Continental Dæk Danmark. Continental is the most successful tire in the WorldTour peloton, from Tadej Pogačar in Milan-Sanremo to Tobias Lund in Wevelgem. Safety starts with the tire, both on the highway and on the bike path. We look back at Jonas Vingegaard’s successful, flawless, and convincing 2026 season, most recently with the overall victory and two stage wins in the Volta a Catalunya. Then we turn to E3 and Gent-Wevelgem (yes, we still call it that). Mathieu van der Poel’s hat trick, the hyenas’ hunt for the king lions, Tobias Lund’s impressive second place, and all the observations we’ve made from the one-day races in 2026 leading up to the Tour of Flanders on Sunday. Can anyone really imagine a winner other than Tadej Pogačar or Mathieu van der Poel after what we’ve seen so far? Featuring Per Bausager and Anders Mielke.

item.title: Henk Vogels: “Learn to use your brain, not just your quads”
item.description: In this episode, former rider Henk Vogels joins the Hotseat for a wide-ranging conversation about the state of young talent in the peloton, why he fears riders like Paul Seixas being thrown into the Tour de France at 19, and what he thinks the UCI should actually be spending its time on instead of regulating sock height. He also makes the case for franchising WorldTour teams, explains why finishing the Tour de France earns riders exactly nothing, and asks why a sport watched by millions still cannot get its economic model right. Vogels also reflects on the dark ages of the 1990s, when performance-enhancing practices were rarely discussed openly but were almost never a surprise, often hidden behind the so-called omertà.

item.title: Top 10 With Heavy Legs and Lungs
item.description: Heavy lungs and heavy legs did not stop Mads P from riding to a top-10 finish in Friday’s E3. We take a deep dive into the week’s races and talk about what we really think of Van der Poel’s setup, the dream son-in-law is from Thy, Vingegaard makes it two from two, and where are his teammates? The Butcher conquers Holland, and can we please be allowed to buy Mads P’s black-and-white bull? That and much more in this episode of Lang Distance, brought to you in collaboration with Nitor Energy.

item.title: 409: Jonas Shows Giro Form in Catalonia
item.description: Jonas Vingegaard won the Volta a Catalunya in dominant fashion. Just like in Paris-Nice, it was Vingegaard and everyone else in the general classification. We review the decisive stages and look at the Dane and his rivals’ form ahead of the Giro d’Italia. In Belgium, the cobbled classics continue with strong results, especially for Tobias Lund, and Alpecin’s dominance continues; Mathieu van der Poel and Jasper Philipsen have now won all four WorldTour classics in Flanders this season. Mads P took on the cobbles in E3, but later had to report sick ahead of Gent-Wevelgem. And there were also a couple of tactical decisions from two Norwegians that did not go entirely unnoticed over the weekend. Veloropa Podcast is presented by betting provider Betano (betano.dk), the Danish supplement brand Aioss (save 300 kroner on your first delivery with the discount code veloropa at aioss.dk), and electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk).

item.title: Is Van der Poel’s Current Level High Enough to Challenge Pogačar This Spring?
item.description: Spencer Martin and Johan Bruyneel break down Mathieu van der Poel’s third straight win at E3, where he narrowly held off a chasing group in a thrilling final kilometer, as well as what we learned from Jonas Vingegaard’s dominant performance on the first summit finish at Volta a Catalunya. They debate what Van der Poel’s late fade could mean for his Monument campaign this spring, and why Remco Evenepoel’s ability to contend for stage-race wins appears to have declined in recent years. item.description: It’s not exactly been cycling’s best-kept secret, but yes, G will be staying on with the INEOS Grenadiers next season as their new Director of Racing. He chatted to Luke all about the role... Listen to What’s G’s new job with the INEOS Grenadiers? He reveals all | Watts Occurring by Watts Occurring instantly on your tablet, phone, or browser - no downloads needed.

item.title: 408: Cort Wins, New Beef Between Remco and Jonas
item.description: It got dramatic when Remco Evenepoel suddenly attacked from the favorites’ group on what seemed like a harmless stage in the Volta a Catalunya. The only rider who could follow him was Jonas Vingegaard. But the Dane did not want to play along, so Remco got angry, and thoughts went back to the Tour de France, where the two rivals had a similar controversy. Fate had it that Remco crashed shortly before the finish and Vingegaard stopped pedaling, even though a stage win was potentially there for the taking. We discuss the situation, Jonas and Remco’s actions, and look ahead to the coming stages in Catalonia, where we have already seen a Danish victory. Magnus Cort was the fastest on stage 2 of the Spanish race after a long spell without wins and health problems. Of course, we also look ahead to the weekend’s cobbled classics, E3 Saxo Classic and Gent-Wevelgem — or as it is now called: In Flanders Fields - From Middelkerke to Wevelgem. Veloropa Podcast is presented in collaboration with betting provider Betano (betano.dk), the Danish supplement brand Aioss (use the code veloropa at aioss.dk and get 300 kroner off your first order), and electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk).

item.title: Milan-San Remo Review, E3 Saxo and Gent-Wevelgem Preview
item.description: The Spring Classics are here. Bram and Ethan review a dramatic Milan-Sanremo, including Pogačar’s crash, his 12-second recovery, and power data from Velon that puts the numbers behind the ride. They also look ahead to E3 Saxo, where Mathieu van der Poel arrives as the clear favorite, and Gent-Wevelgem, now officially renamed In Flanders Fields - From Middelkerke to Wevelgem, though the debate over what to actually call it opens the episode. Volta a Catalunya, Evenepoel’s Grand Tour credentials, and Van Aert’s Roubaix chances also feature. This episode is fueled by MNSTRY. item.title: The Ambition to Win
item.description: A little late, but we’re back with a deep dive into San Remo, plus all the other Danish victories since we last spoke. Clear weekly highlights for Valgren, Jakob has plenty of weekly blunders, what awaits Mads now, and why didn’t Del Toro close down Van Aert? That and much more in this episode, brought to you in collaboration with Nitor Energy.

item.title: S1 Ep689: It’s Official: Tadej Pogačar Is the GOAT. Here’s Why
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we tackle the ultimate question in cycling: after his incredible Milano-Sanremo victory, is Tadej Pogačar already the greatest of all time? We also take a look at the stunning and expensive collaboration between Factor and Bugatti, alongside the latest tech news including 32-inch mountain bike wheels and new rubber from Pirelli. Finally, we catch up on the latest headlines from the pro peloton, including Nairo Quintana’s retirement and the trial concerning Alejandro Valverde.

item.title: Milano-Sanremo 2026
item.description: Forhjulslir is presented in collaboration with Continental Dæk Danmark. Continental is the most successful tire in the WorldTour peloton, from Tobias Lund in Tirreno to Tadej Pogačar in Milano-Sanremo. Safety starts with the tire, both on the highway and on the bike path. Tadej Pogačar did it. He solved Liguria’s puzzle, won Milano-Sanremo, and claimed his fourth of the five Monuments. In a historic edition where everything went against the world champion. In today’s episode of Forhjulslir, we take a close look at Saturday’s Milano-Sanremo and dive into all the elements of the thrilling finale. Bausager reads the 2026 edition of the message in a bottle, with new twists and impressions. We also briefly stop by the Tour of Catalonia and finally E3 and the Flemish cobbles waiting this weekend. Without Pogačar, but with Mads P, Mathieu van der Poel, and maybe Wout van Aert. Featuring: Per Bausager and Anders Mielke

How do I avoid muscle cramps in longer 4-5+ hour events? How do you prepare for long rides? How do you balance weekday training and rides? Tips for someone new to mountain biking? I'm nervous. How do I get faster on my road bike? item.title: Monuments: Paris-Roubaix
item.description: Paris-Roubaix. The Hell of the North, l’Enfer du Nord, is a mythical race. In this episode of Bagerstop’s special Monument series, you can get closer to Paris-Roubaix. From the smoky room at the velodrome in Roubaix, where the race was first mapped out, to today’s gladiator battles on the cobbles in the Arenberg Forest. The episode’s experts are Brian Holm, Kasper Asgreen, Johan Museeuw, and Bastian Emil Goldschmidt. Support the show

item.title: 407: Pogačar’s Crazy Triumph - Jonas in a Mountain Battle with Remco
item.description: Tadej Pogačar crashed just as this year’s first Monument, Milano-Sanremo, was about to be decided. But 10 km later he attacked as planned on the Cipressa climb, and on the Poggio he was left alone with Tom Pidcock, whom the dominant Slovenian beat in the sprint on Via Roma. Pogačar’s first Sanremo victory, and what a win it was! It was also his fourth Monument out of five possible. We look back at a crazy edition of the Spring Classic, where Mads P also made a sensational comeback to finish fourth and Mathieu van der Poel, for once, ran out of energy. The Tour of Catalonia has just begun, and Pidcock was again in the spotlight there, but he had to settle for third on the opening stage, which was won by INEOS French champion Dorian Godon ahead of Remco Evenepoel, whom most consider Jonas Vingegaard’s biggest rival in the fight for the overall victory. We look ahead to the coming stages in Catalonia and examine the route, the favorites, and the Danes’ chances of success. item.description: It’s the day after. The year’s first Monument has been raced. The Gruppetto is gathered in La Turbie, 40 km from Via Roma and yesterday’s thrilling finish to Milano-Sanremo 2026. Hear Matson speak honestly about the brutal battle for position, Mads P’s comeback, and all the impressions from the biggest cycling Saturday of the season so far. item.title: Nick Schultz Breaks Down Israel-Premier Tech Drama
item.description: Nick Schultz joins The Hotseat to look back on a career that has taken him from Brisbane to Caja Rural, GreenEdge, Israel-Premier Tech, and now NSN Cycling Team — and to look ahead to Milan-Sanremo and the 2026 Giro d'Italia. The Australian domestique talks about the BB Hotels KTM collapse that left him without a team in December, what he learned riding alongside Chris Froome, the political pressure of racing for Israel Premier Tech in 2025, and what it actually felt like to beat Tadej Pogačar in a bunch sprint. He also shares his view on how nutrition and race speeds have changed the peloton since 2017, why he avoided the Tour Down Under for the first decade of his career, and what he makes of the culture shift from IPT to NSN.

item.title: The Greatest Race in Modern Cycling? | Milan-Sanremo 2026 Breakdown
item.description: George Hincapié and Spencer Martin break down Tadej Pogačar's win at the 2026 Milan-Sanremo, where he captured one of the only Monuments he had yet to win after recovering from a hard crash in the finale and delivering one of the greatest comeback performances in modern cycling history. They discuss the key moments of the race, how the crash may have actually helped UAE's plan to attack on the Cipressa, the standout rides behind him, and what rivals can do to stop Pogačar in the upcoming Cobbled Classics. item.description: Sports broadcaster and journalist Anders Mielke joins Matt Stephens to share his journey from junior cyclist in Denmark to one of the most exciting young voices in cycling media. Anders reflects on the moment he realized a pro career wasn’t for him, how he built a podcast from scratch, and the path that led him to the commentary booth. He also lifts the lid on creating Chasing Cobbles, his behind-the-scenes documentary with Mads Pedersen, offering a unique insight into trust, storytelling, and life inside the pro peloton. item.title: 153. Aero mutinies and secret shorts with Castelli's Steve Smith
item.description:
This week, Will and James are joined by Castelli and Sportful global brand manager Steve Smith. Steve grew up in Portland, USA, where he, like most people in the city, worked for Nike for 10 years. In 2000, he made a bold move to Italy to live the bella vita with a job at Sportful. Four years later, parent company Manifattura Valcismon bought Castelli, and since then Steve and his team have helped create some of cycling’s biggest game-changers, from some of the earliest wind-tunnel-designed aero kit to the now-famous Castelli Gabba, which transformed foul-weather cycling gear and helped create an entirely new clothing category.
In this conversation, Steve explains how those early aero jerseys sparked a mutiny among Castelli’s sponsored pros; the day Castelli stunned everyone, including the race commissaires, with turquoise shorts; what the fastest kit would really look like if riders could only put it on properly; what the changes in PFC chemical regulations have really meant for cycling clothing; and how waterproof kit actually works, and why some jackets eventually stop working while others stay waterproof forever.
Our chat with Steve begins at 10:34.
This episode is brought to you by the Hammerhead Karoo GPS bike computer. Visit hammerhead.io and use the code CYCLIST to get a free HR strap with every purchase. Just be sure to add the strap to your cart before applying the code at checkout.

item.title: 406: Pogacar and Van der Poel ready for another Sanremo duel
item.description:
On Saturday, this year’s first Monument, Milan-Sanremo, will be raced. At nearly 300 kilometers, it marks the arrival of spring and the ride down to the Mediterranean, where Tadej Pogacar and Mathieu van der Poel are expected to battle again on the two decisive climbs, the Cipressa and the Poggio.
But two of the outsiders for victory, Tom Pidcock and Jasper Philipsen, got a strong lead-in to La Classicissima by winning the warm-up races Milano-Torino and Nokere Koerse. We look at the other favorites and the Danish chances in Milan-Sanremo, and of course we also cover the race’s history, route, and tactical possibilities.
We also have our quiz, and once again there is so much trouble with the young Djurhuus that quizmaster Claus Elmgreen feels forced to hand out a yellow card - very much in the spirit of modern cycling.
Veloropa Podcast is presented in collaboration with betting provider Betano (betano.dk), meal kits from HelloFresh (hellofresh.dk), and electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk). item.title: Milan-Sanremo preview and Vingegaard’s Paris-Nice triumph
item.description:
Forhjulslir is presented in collaboration with Continental Tyres Denmark. Continental is the most successful tire brand in the WorldTour peloton, from Tadej Pogačar in the rainbow jersey to Tobias Lund in Tirreno. Safety starts with the tire - both on the motorway and on the bike path.
Tobias Lund, Jonas Vingegaard, and Michael Valgren delivered a historic Danish week on the WorldTour. Lund sprinted into the elite of the world’s best sprinters, Valgren finally tasted victory again, and Vingegaard flew from the season opener. Yellow, polka dots, and green. Two stage wins and the overall victory in Paris-Nice.
We review the final halves of Tirreno and Paris-Nice, and the Danish victories. Finally, we look ahead in more detail to Saturday’s first Monument of the year. La Primavera - Milan-Sanremo: the Cipressa, the Poggio, the red phone box, and Via Roma. Will the sixth time be the charm for Tadej Pogacar in the Monument that is easiest to finish and hardest to win?
Featuring Per Bausager and Anders Mielke.

item.title: S1 Ep688: The 5 Secret Habits Of Every Cyclist!
item.description:
This week on the GCN Show, we’re celebrating a huge new World Motorpaced Hour Record and the launch of Cannondale’s latest aluminum bike, the CAAD14. We also dive into the strange habits that unite all cyclists - from checking reflections in shop windows to the dreaded Lycra school run - and discuss the latest pro racing action from Paris-Nice and Tirreno-Adriatico.
Plus, Dan and Si have a big announcement about a trip to Australia for a very special gravel race!

item.title: Monuments: Tour of Flanders
item.description:
Oude Kwaremont, Koppenberg, and Muur van Geraardsbergen. Few races have more iconic landmarks than the Tour of Flanders, and nowhere are there more people lining the roadside than at De Ronde.
In this second episode of Bagerstop’s Monument series, you can get up close to the legendary race - its history, its challenges, and the Belgian spectators. The episode also features two former winners of the race.
The words in this episode come from Kasper Asgreen, Brian Holm, Bastian Emil Goldschmidt, and Johan Museeuw.
Support the show.

item.title: Did This Weekend Change the GC Hierarchy? | Paris-Nice/Tirreno-Adriatico Breakdown
item.description:
Spencer Martin and Johan Bruyneel break down the past weekend of stage racing, from Isaac del Toro’s overall win at Tirreno-Adriatico to Jonas Vingegaard’s dominant ride at Paris-Nice. They discuss what to make of Del Toro’s effortless win in Italy, Jorgenson’s strong ride to second, and Mathieu van der Poel’s destruction of the peloton while training for next weekend’s Milan-Sanremo.
They also consider what Jonas Vingegaard’s win at Paris-Nice really means, and what his inability to drop Lenny Martinez on the final stage says about his Grand Tour ambitions later this year.

item.title: Can Tadej Pogacar drop Van der Poel on the Cipressa?
item.description:
Can anyone beat Pogacar and Van der Poel at Milan-Sanremo? After what we saw in Tirreno and Paris-Nice this week, it’s going to be a fight. Vingegaard crushed Paris-Nice. Del Toro won Tirreno at 22. Van der Poel is in frightening form. And everyone wants to sign Paul Seixas.
We break it all down and preview Saturday’s first Monument of the season.

item.title: #96 The Gruppetto on Forhjulslir: Worre’s good advice
item.description:
The sun has come to Nice after a couple of dreadful rainy training rides for Matson and Mads P. Milan-Sanremo is approaching, and the Gruppetto is looking forward to being reunited next weekend. Shout-out to Valgren and Axel - and remember to watch on YouTube if you want to see Mielke’s new haircut.
Featuring Anders Mielke and Mathias Sunekær Norsgaard.

item.title: Long live Accountant Preben
item.description:
We apologize many times for giving Henning from BDO a new nickname, but we also thank BDO for the dance. Accountant Preben delivers some gems in this episode and answers the question of whether he has a connection to Papa P.
We discuss Strade, more love for Del Toro, Visma and Vingegaard, the blunder of the week, and a happy man is two toilets and two refrigerators - all in this episode of Lang Distance, brought to you in collaboration with BDO Denmark.

item.title: 405: 5 wins in 5 days: A perfect week for Denmark
item.description:
Jonas Vingegaard became the first Dane to win Paris-Nice overall, and he also took two impressive stage wins along the way. Tobias Lund Andresen won a sprint stage in Tirreno-Adriatico even though most of the world’s best sprinters were in the race. And last but certainly not least, Michael Valgren took his first victory since the terrible crash in 2022 on stage 5 of Tirreno-Adriatico.
We look back on a week of major Danish triumphs, which also saw Casper Pedersen enjoy wearing the mountains jersey in Paris-Nice on almost every stage, and Jonas Vingegaard ultimately take home three of the race’s leader jerseys. We also look ahead to next week’s warm-up races for Milan-Sanremo - the first Monument of the year, which awaits next weekend.
Veloropa Podcast is presented in collaboration with betting provider Betano (betano.dk), the Danish supplement brand Aioss (use discount code veloropa at aioss.dk and save DKK 100 on each of the first 3 months), and electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk).

item.title: Alex Carera: The man behind Tadej Pogačar and Isaac del Toro
item.description: One of cycling’s most influential agents joins us on The Hotseat. Alex Carera, founder of the A&J All Sports agency and longtime manager of some of the sport’s biggest stars, including Tadej Pogačar, talks about talent scouting, guiding young riders through the chaos of the WorldTour, and the realities of managing more than 100 professional cyclists around the world. From protecting riders under the pressure of the Tour de France to negotiating long contracts and navigating cycling’s changing transfer market, Carera offers a rare look inside the business side of the peloton and the responsibilities that come with shaping a champion’s career. Also in this episode: how Carera built one of cycling’s largest rider management agencies; what happened behind the scenes during Pogačar’s 2025 Tour de France; why UAE Team Emirates is a long-term home for GC talent like Isaac del Toro; the growing debate around long contracts and buyout clauses in cycling; Carera’s view on a possible transfer market in professional cycling; the story behind controversial crypto sponsorship deals in the sport; why Pogačar uses personal sponsorship money to fund youth teams and charity; and the growing safety concerns when pro riders train on public roads.

item.title: Is Jonas Vingegaard the best GC rider right now? And what is Paul Seixas’ market value?
item.description: Spencer Martin and Johan Bruyneel break down the latest week of cycling action, from Jonas Vingegaard’s dominance at Paris-Nice to Isaac del Toro’s impressive control of the GC field at Tirreno-Adriatico, and discuss what Mathieu van der Poel and Wout van Aert’s form is telling us about the upcoming Spring Classics. They debate Vingegaard’s prospects at the Giro and the Tour based on his recent form, and what salary teenage French star Paul Seixas, who is out of contract at the end of 2027, will command when he reaches the market. item.title: Vingegaard and Lund’s wild Wednesday
item.description: Forhjulslir is presented in collaboration with Continental Dæk Danmark. Continental is the most successful tire in the WorldTour peloton, from Tadej Pogačar in the rainbow jersey to Tobias Lund in Tirreno. Safety starts with the tire — both on the highway and on the bike path. Tadej Pogačar did it again, and again, and again. On Saturday he rode in, in rainbow, with dust hanging over him, onto Piazza del Campo for his fourth Strade Bianche victory. Another display of power — from the first start, pedal stroke, and finish line in the 2026 season. In today’s episode of Forhjulslir, we look back at Strade, Tadej Pogačar, his new 19-year-old challenger Paul Seixas, and of course Jonas and Tobias. Vingegaard and Lund. Like yesterday, Wednesday, they each took WorldTour stage wins in Paris-Nice and Tirreno-Adriatico. Featuring: Per Bausager, Emil Mielke Vinjebo, and Anders Mielke.

item.title: 404: Strade Pogačar - Jonas chasing in France
item.description: Same procedure as last year... Tadej Pogačar was once again dominant at Strade Bianche, which he won in record time for the fourth time in his career and the third time in a row. We discuss whether Pogačar’s dominance is killing cycling, or whether we should simply be happy to witness something historic. In France, Jonas Vingegaard has to chase in the mountains after Visma did not live up to expectations in Tuesday’s team time trial. Vingegaard is now 17 seconds behind his presumed biggest rival, Juan Ayuso of Lidl-Trek, who leads Paris-Nice by a few seconds ahead of the INEOS duo Kevin Vauquelin and Oscar Onley. On the positive side, Casper P has taken the race’s polka-dot mountain jersey. In Italy, Filippo Ganna once again won the opening time trial in Tirreno-Adriatico, while Mathieu van der Poel took the stage 2 win on the gravel finish up to the old town in San Gimignano. There is also a quiz, and we revisit the time Kasper Asgreen beat Tadej Pogačar at the Istrian Spring Trophy and when Elming jinxed Michael Matthews badly last week. Veloropa Podcast is presented in collaboration with betting provider Betano (betano.dk), meal kits from HelloFresh (hellofresh.dk), and electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk).

item.title: #95 Gruppettoen on Forhjulslir: Back in Nice
item.description: It became Aarhus-Nice rather than Paris-Nice for Matson, where he will spend the next little under two weeks as a punching bag for Mads P. We look back at the week that passed, the first training session in Nice, sinuses, cars, and chaos. Featuring: Anders Mielke and Mathias Sunekær Norsgaard. item.title: S1 Ep687: Why a heavier cyclist is a better cyclist
item.description: Tadej Pogačar looks unstoppable after his Strade Bianche masterclass, but is his new beefier physique the secret to his success? This week on the GCN Show, we dive into the data suggesting that gaining weight might actually be the next big marginal gain in pro cycling. We also check out five brand-new bike launches from Giant, Merida, and Bianchi, find out which bike Ed Sheeran just bought, and Dan compares his Strava times to Pogačar on the infamous Le Tolfe climb!

item.title: Ayuso vs. Vingegaard in France and del Toro vs. Roglič in Italy
item.description: Tadej Pogačar did it again: a 79-kilometre solo ride into Siena, a fourth Strade Bianche title, and a peloton left wondering what the point is. Bram and Ethan dig into the race, the battle for second between Paul Seixas and Isaac del Toro, and why the women’s race was frankly more entertaining. Then: two of the biggest stage races of the early season are underway. Jonas Vingegaard makes his 2026 debut at Paris-Nice with a disrupted winter behind him. At Tirreno-Adriatico, del Toro leads UAE Team Emirates-XRG without Pogačar — and that could get complicated. Roglič, Ayuso, Pellizzari, and a route built for punchers. Who wins what?

item.title: Fueling for success on the bike with Uri Carlson
item.description: In this interview, I chat with nutritionist Uri Carlson about how to fuel in order to feel strong and fast on the bike. We discuss how many carbs you should be eating on the bike, real food versus sports nutrition products, and fueling at different life stages. 🔥 $50 off training plans with coupon code PODCAST50. 👉 https://shop.femmecyclist.com/product-category/training-plans/ ❤️ Join Kristen and The Femme Cyclist Community ➡︎ Website: https://femmecyclist.com ➡︎ Instagram: https://instagram.com/femme_cyclist ➡︎ Facebook: https://facebook.com/femmecyclist 💜 Connect with Uri ➡︎ Website: https://www.innerwildnutrition.com/ ➡︎ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/uri_carlson_nutrition/ Support the show 👉 Instagram: @femme_cyclist 👉 Website: https://femmecyclist.com

item.title: The Monuments: Milan-San Remo
item.description: An episode about Milan-San Remo. Cycling’s Monuments, along with the World Championships, are the biggest one-day races on the entire calendar. That is why the world’s best riders arrive in top form for these five races, willing to risk life and limb to raise their arms at the finish line. In this first of five episodes, Bagerstop zooms in on Milan-San Remo. La Primavera. The harbinger of spring. With Brian Holm, Bastian Emil Goldschmidt, and Kasper Asgreen at the microphones, you can learn more about the history, anatomy, and brutality of Milan-San Remo — cycling’s longest one-day race. These five episodes about cycling’s Monuments are presented in collaboration with Bygma, the largest Danish-owned supplier to the construction industry, which always has the popular Airtox on the shelves. Support the show.

item.title: A love affair with Del Toro
item.description: The hottest episode in a long time, and that is of course because we have BDO visiting in the form of Henning, better known as Revisor Preben, and has nothing to do with the Mexican Grand Prix slacker DJ Preben made in honor of Mads P’s man crush. We answer listener questions for Henning, who, to be fair, is chief consultant at BDO and knows everything about what can be deducted, among other things. Listen in and go far and wide with us in this episode brought in collaboration with BDO Danmark — which turned out to be an international company with 110,000 employees. A big apology from our side.

item.title: Did the Future of Pro Cycling Just Arrive? | Strade Bianche Breakdown
item.description: George Hincapie, Johan Bruyneel, and Spencer Martin break down Saturday’s Strade Bianche, which took the peloton through the rolling hills and white gravel roads of Tuscany, where Tadej Pogačar battled rising talent Paul Seixas to claim his fourth solo victory in the last five editions of the race. The crew discusses how and why Pogačar keeps making the same attack work, and whether the fight for the podium suggests this dynamic could soon change. item.title: Is Paul Seixas Already a Top-5 Rider? | Strade Bianche Preview
item.description: Spencer Martin and Johan Bruyneel break down the course, contenders, and likely outcome of Saturday’s Strade Bianche, which features a showdown between World Champion Tadej Pogačar and rising star Paul Seixas. Beyond asking how anyone can compete with UAE’s previously unbeatable formula at this race, they also look ahead to next week’s GC battle at Paris-Nice between Jonas Vingegaard and Juan Ayuso, plus a star-studded edition of Tirreno-Adriatico. item.title: Imogen Cotter on Resilience, Reinvention, and Life Beyond the Peloton
item.description: Former Irish road race national champion Imogen Cotter joins Matt Stephens to discuss a cycling journey defined by grit, ambition, and resilience. From funding her racing career while working as a postwoman in Belgium to winning the Irish National Championships in 2021 and signing her first professional contract, Cotter’s rise was hard-earned and rapid. But just weeks into life as a pro, a serious collision while training in Girona changed everything. In this episode, Imogen speaks candidly about recovery, early retirement, rediscovering her love for cycling, and pursuing a new path in sports journalism. A thoughtful and inspiring conversation released ahead of International Women’s Day. Read the accompanying article on the Sigma Sports Hub: https://www.sigmasports.com/hub/news/imogen-cotter-resilience-and-reinvention www.sigmasports.com Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices

item.title: Simon Geschke: 'I don't want to see that polka dot jersey'
item.description: Are you enjoying our podcast? Please help us by giving it a 5-star rating, as it really helps people find us. Former German cyclist Simon Geschke joins Aidan to reflect on a 16-year WorldTour career, the evolution of cycling technology, and the moments that defined his life in the peloton. From his breakthrough Tour de France stage win in 2015 to wearing the polka dot jersey in 2022, Geschke shares what it really feels like to compete at the highest level and why some results still sting years later. He also reflects on how the sport changed during his career with the rise of disc brakes, power meters, and increasingly aerodynamic bikes, and breaks down the instinct and timing behind his 2015 Tour de France stage win. Geschke also talks about retirement, becoming a father, and figuring out what life looks like after professional cycling, while still staying close to the sport through coaching and media work.

item.title: 403: Season Debut for Pogačar and Vingegaard
item.description: Tadej Pogačar starts Saturday as a three-time winner of the modern classic Strade Bianche, while Jonas Vingegaard makes his season debut on Sunday in the stage race Paris-Nice. Both appear to face tough opposition, with Pogačar up against French rising star Paul Seixas and Vingegaard set to meet former teammates João Almeida and Juan Ayuso in the Race to the Sun. We look ahead to the two world stars’ first appearances of the season, including the favorites, routes, Danish riders, and much more. We also look back at the weekend’s Belgian cobbled openers, Omloop Nieuwsblad and Kuurne-Brussels-Kuurne, where Mathieu van der Poel took a convincing win, rising star Matt Brennan again showed his class, and Danish rider Tobias Lund truly broke onto the classics scene in a weekend when many riders hit the deck and several, including Belgian champion Tim Wellens, saw their spring ambitions fade. Veloropa Podcast is presented in collaboration with betting provider Betano (betano.dk), meal kits from HelloFresh (hellofresh.dk), and electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk).

item.title: 152. Zoe Bäckstedt: Why I Love Sundays in Hell
item.description: This week, Robyn and James are joined by WorldTour pro Zoe Bäckstedt. Born in 2004 in Pontyclun, Wales, to British track champion Megan Hughes and Paris-Roubaix winner Magnus Bäckstedt, there were really only two paths for Zoe’s life, and her sister Elynor’s too: cycling. She burst onto the scene as a teenager, winning multiple junior world titles on the road, track, and in cyclocross, before turning pro with EF Education-Tibco-SVB in 2022. The following season brought a move to Canyon//Sram zondacrypto, where she took her first pro win a season later with a stage victory at the Simac Ladies Tour. Strong results have continued across all three disciplines, but at just 21, colleagues, managers, and pundits all agree on one thing: Zoe’s best is still ahead of her. In this episode, Zoe talks about how her multi-discipline background has shaped her into a Classics specialist; how her dad Magnus and mum Megan, along with the British rain, helped make her the rider she is; why Paris-Roubaix remains her favorite race despite the chaos and carnage; and how hard it is to play board games in such a competitive family. Zoe’s chat begins at 7:38. This episode is brought to you by the Hammerhead Karoo GPS bike computer. item.title: Omloop au naturel and Spring Classics with Carl-Frederik Bévort and Niklas Larsen
item.description: Radio Tour is back on Danish soil, and with two hard men. Carl-Frederik Bévort and Niklas Larsen come straight from the heat of battle in Omloop, with mud, cobbles, and cold fingers.

item.title: Radio Tour: Omloop au naturel and Spring Classics with Carl-Frederik Bévort and Niklas Larsen
item.description: Radio Tour is back on Danish soil, and with two hard men. Carl-Frederik Bévort and Niklas Larsen come straight from the heat of battle in Omloop, with mud, cobbles, and cold fingers.

item.title: Opening Weekend & Strade Bianche
item.description: Forhjulslir is presented in collaboration with Continental Tyres Denmark. Continental is the most successful tire brand in the WorldTour peloton, from Tadej Pogačar in the rainbow jersey to Paul Seixas and Tobias Lund on Decathlon. Safety starts with the tire, both on the highway and on the bike path. Spring is knocking at the door. Mathieu van der Poel opened the Flemish classics season in Omloop, Matthew Brennan sprinted to victory for Visma in Kuurne, and Tobias Lund had his classics breakthrough in the Belgian opening weekend. There is plenty to dig into from the weekend and plenty to look forward to, perhaps cycling’s most beautiful race, on Saturday in the heart of Tuscany: Strade Bianche. We try to prepare you for the white roads of Tuscany and the battle between Pogačar and Paul. Featuring Per Bausager, Emil Mielke Vinjebo, and Anders Mielke.

item.title: Mission: Build the Coolest Bagerstop Rocket on a Budget
item.description: Bagerstop has gotten hold of a carbon frame at a sharp price. Now the mission is to make it as cool as possible, for as little money as possible. And it all has to happen with help from Bagerstop’s friends. Listen in and hear how great a bike we can build for 17,500 kroner. The two experts and bike mechanics judging the purchases are Victor Malgaard Ottensen from Scandinavian Bike Tech and Kasper Sømod Jensen from Bings Bikestore. Maybe you’ll get inspiration or useful tips on how to upgrade your current bike in the best possible way. Support the show

item.title: #94 Gruppettoen on Forhjulslir: Post Opening Weekend
item.description: Gruppettoen is back with reflections and reactions from the Belgian Opening Weekend. From flight chaos, the smell of warming cream, and Mielke’s first day in the field on Saturday at Omloop, to somber faces and disappointment at Lidl-Trek on Sunday in Kuurne. And everything in between: bikefitter cousin Andreas, watt records, police duties, and much more. Featuring Anders Mielke and Mathias Sunekær Norsgaard. item.title: S1 Ep686: Are Pogačar and Van der Poel from Another Planet?
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we dive into the incredible dominance of riders like Tadej Pogačar and Mathieu van der Poel, asking whether they are truly "extraterrestrial" compared with the rest of the peloton. We also take a first look at the brand-new Shimano Tiagra groupset and discuss a controversial UCI disqualification involving bent brake levers. Plus, we celebrate a huge milestone of 1 million bikes with World Bicycle Relief and unbox a very special surprise from YouTube!

item.title: Van der Poel Dominates as Brennan and Seixas Announce Themselves | Opening Weekend Breakdown
item.description: Spencer Martin and Johan Bruyneel break down the opening weekend of the Flemish cobbled Classics, starting with Mathieu van der Poel’s dominant victory on Saturday at Omloop Nieuwsblad and Mathew Brennan and Visma’s statement win at Kuurne-Brussel-Kuurne on Sunday. They then discuss what to make of Paul Seixas riding the field off his wheel at Faun-Ardèche and tying Tadej Pogačar’s climbing record in the process. item.description: This week’s episode is brought to you in collaboration with BDO Denmark, and for that reason Mads P naturally has a financial tip 😅 We get a visit from Roglic, or is it just Mads who has been out on a trip with the trimmer? 🤔 We also stop by Frank Hvam on Rouvy, show some love for new windshield wipers, discuss whether to use a bidet or not, and bring in some Faxe cans and helmets for Uno. item.title: Mathieu van der Poel's Surprise Season Debut | Omloop and Kuurne Preview
item.description: Spencer Martin and Johan Bruyneel look ahead to cycling’s opening Spring Classics weekend, with Mathieu van der Poel making his season debut at Saturday’s Omloop Nieuwsblad and the sport’s best sprinters facing off at Sunday’s Kuurne-Brussels-Kuurne. They discuss who they think will win, share the riders and storylines they’ll be watching, and then speed-rank the 10 best riders of all time. item.title: 402: Van der Poel Ready, Wout Out - Previewing the Belgian Opening Weekend
item.description: The day after Mathieu van der Poel confirmed he was ready for the Belgian Opening Weekend, Wout van Aert withdrew because of illness. Jonas Vingegaard will race Paris-Nice instead of the UAE Tour, which was won by Isaac Del Toro. In Paris-Nice, Vingegaard avoids both Del Toro and Pogačar, but will instead face Juan Ayuso, who made an impressive debut for Lidl-Trek with the overall victory in a strong Algarve Tour. We look ahead to the weekend’s Omloop and Kuurne-Brussels-Kuurne with favorites, route analysis, stars, and Danish riders — and the question is whether Stefan can get revenge for last week’s quiz embarrassment. Veloropa Podcast is presented in collaboration with betting provider Betano (betano.dk), meal kits from HelloFresh (hellofresh.dk), and electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk).

item.title: S1 Ep685: "Cycling Has Never Been So Cheap" (WTF?!)
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we dive into the supposedly prohibitive cost of cycling and investigate whether the sport is actually cheaper than ever thanks to a booming second-hand market. We also explore a bizarre new scientific study suggesting that training in a hot tub could significantly boost your FTP. Plus, Dan recounts the moment he was overtaken by a World Champion while riding flat out, and we take a look at Shimano’s latest self-tightening shoe patent.

item.title: UAE, Algarve, and Omloop Preview
item.description: Forhjulslir is presented in collaboration with Continental Dæk Danmark. Continental is the most winning tire in the WorldTour peloton, from Tadej Pogačar in the rainbow jersey to Isaac del Toro in the desert. Safety starts with the tire — both on the highway and on the bike path. Isaac del Toro headbutted Remco Evenepoel in the desert. Juan Ayuso delivered and lifted Lidl-Trek to the top in Algarve, and the two French prodigies, Paul & Paul, Seixas & Magnier, shone again. Spanish champion Ivan Romeo took control in Andalusia, and Tom Pidcock found exactly the right punch heading into Opening Weekend. The exams have been taken. Some riders got top marks and peace of mind before Opening Weekend, Paris-Nice, and Tirreno-Adriatico. Others are left with more questions than answers. In this episode of Forhjulslir, we review the week’s big performances, the hidden signals in the results, and turn our attention to the holy Flemish opening celebration with Omloop Het Nieuwsblad. We also have Emil Mielke Vinjebo on the line for a chat about Faxe Kondi, Ruta del Sol, and Uno-X’s expectations for Omloop as defending winners. Featuring Per Bausager and Anders Mielke.

item.title: Bikes, Art, and Advocacy With Lisa Congdon
item.description: Lisa Congdon is one of my favorite people to follow online. Her colorful art, love of bikes, and courage in speaking up for causes she believes in are all deeply inspiring to me. At 58, Lisa is a shining example of what’s possible in the second half of our lives. She started riding bikes consistently in her 40s and didn’t start racing until her 50s. In this podcast episode, we discuss what drew Lisa to endurance racing, how working with a coach transformed her relationship with cycling, and why she believes older women are uniquely suited for long-distance events. We also talk about why Lisa chose to show up as her whole self online — art, cycling, activism, and all — and how speaking out about her values has actually grown both her following and her opportunities, rather than diminished them. ⭐ Sponsored by Cycle Limited. item.title: #93 Gruppettoen on Forhjulslir: Mallorca Camp
item.description: Matson has no hesitation in calling the Mallorca camp the best training camp of his career. Hear why on Gruppettoen, where you also get a detailed look at the training camp setup, the activities, Mads P’s rehab, and Claus P’s ambitious watts-per-kilo attempt. Featuring: Anders Mielke and Mathias Sunekær Norsgaard. Gruppettoen on Forhjulslir is sponsored by Aioss. item.title: Did We Just See Pro Cycling's Future? | UAE Tour, Algarve & Ruta del Sol
item.description: Spencer Martin and Johan Bruyneel break down the past weekend of racing, from Isaac del Toro’s impressive win at the UAE Tour to Juan Ayuso’s duel with Paul Seixas at Volta ao Algarve, and what we learned from it all. They discuss Del Toro’s record-breaking ascent of Jebel Hafeet, Remco’s disappointing final GC result, Jonas Vingegaard’s revised spring schedule, and whether Decathlon should send 19-year-old Paul Seixas to the Tour de France. item.title: Mads P on the trainer
item.description: Come inside the workshop and see what it looks like to have Mads P on the trainer. We caught Mads in the middle of his five-hour “torture session” on an improvised home-trainer setup. It’s an action-packed episode with a parasol crash, Søren KA in a painter’s outfit, another weekly blunder from Jan Christen, whether you’re even allowed to have a Mexican man crush, and much more.

item.description: Lizzie is three days away from giving birth, Ned is at the Manchester Velodrome, and David and Pete are where they are. Redeem your AG1 offer here! item.title: Remco's Stage Race Struggles Continue as Del Toro and Seixas Rise
item.description: Spencer Martin and Johan Bruyneel break down the recent racing, including Paul Seixas’s impressive stage win at Volta ao Algarve over Juan Ayuso and João Almeida, and the UAE Tour, where Isaac del Toro has shown impressive form while Remco Evenepoel continues to struggle with stage-race consistency. They discuss what may be behind Evenepoel being dropped by the same group of riders he attacked just last weekend, and what it means for Red Bull’s GC plans later in the season. They also examine the recent revelation that Visma–Lease a Bike is looking for a new title sponsor, and what it says about the sport’s financial instability and the ever-rising salaries of its star riders. item.title: Stefan Küng on Chasing Monuments, Precision, and a New Chapter at Tudor Pro Cycling
item.description: Swiss powerhouse Stefan Küng joins Matt Stephens from Tudor Pro Cycling’s training camp in Spain to reflect on a career built on consistency, curiosity, and quiet ambition. Küng opens up about leaving Groupama-FDJ for a new challenge, the vision behind Tudor’s project, and why success in the Classics still drives him. The conversation explores racing on instinct in an era of marginal gains, evolving nutrition and technology, and the balance between innovation and safety in modern cycling. Stefan also shares formative stories from his youth, the people who shaped his self-belief, and a memorable European Championship win before taking on a somewhat unfinished hometown quiz to round things off. www.sigmasports.com Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices

item.title: 401: Remco meltdown, Mads P update, and Paul Seixas
item.description: Remco Evenepoel had an almost perfect start to the season after his move to Red Bull Bora-Hansgrohe. That is, until the UAE Tour peloton tackled the tough Jebel Mobrah on Wednesday. There, the Belgian cracked with cramps and a body that shut down long before the riders reached the top of the steep climb, and Remco lost more than two minutes to winner Antonio Tiberi. Mads P has given an update on his injury situation after the crash in Valenciana on his own podcast. We discuss the Danish star’s chances in the upcoming cobbled Classics. Another star who, according to Stefan in this episode, will be the next French Tour de France winner, 19-year-old Paul Seixas, continues to impress. On Thursday he won a mountain stage in the Volta ao Algarve ahead of strong names such as Juan Ayuso, Joao Almeida and Oscar Onley. Veloropa Podcast is presented in collaboration with Betano (betano.dk), meal kits from Hello Fresh (hellofresh.dk), and electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk).

item.title: 151. UCI rules, design evolution, and why bikes are so expensive, with Pieter Potters
item.description: This week’s guest is Pieter Potters, R&D manager at Belgian Cycling Factory, the group that owns Eddy Merckx Cycles and Ridley Bikes. For nearly six years, Pieter has been at the centre of designing cutting-edge road and gravel bikes, including the recent Ridley Noah Fast 3.0, which features a new kind of geometry that many see as poised to change the game — think steep seat tubes, short cranks, and aggressive forward positioning of the rider (don’t worry, Pieter explains it better). Pieter is also in charge of designing Eddy Merckx bikes, so here he discusses plans to rejuvenate that once-great marque, along with answering questions about how much input pro riders really have into the bikes they ride, how to design bikes for the modern age, how to negotiate potentially ruinous UCI rule changes, and the perennial question: why are bikes so expensive? Interview begins at This episode is brought to you by the Hammerhead Karoo GPS bike computer. item.title: S1 Ep684: Pogačar Reveals His Mind-Blowing Power Zones
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we take a deep dive into Tadej Pogačar’s recently revealed power zones and what his Strava data tells us about his world-class fitness. We also explore the launch of Sir Bradley Wiggins’ new AI coaching platform and the rise of gravel racing super-teams like the new Canyon by DT Swiss squad. Plus, we discuss the latest tech from Trek’s 50th anniversary, the controversial schedule changes for Paris-Roubaix, and our regular Hack or Bodge!

item.title: The Lund Andresen Brothers: World Tour Wins and a Cycling Empire
item.description: Tobias Lund Andresen is one of the hottest names on the World Tour right now, with a Tour de France appearance last year and fresh wins in two World Tour races in Australia. Fewer people know that his brother Andreas also has a very interesting story. Bagerstop spoke with the fast brothers from Taastrup, who in their own ways are taking Danish cycling by storm these years. Support the show

Also hear about the Marseille thugs, the Mallorca camp starting tomorrow, and Mielke’s business-class trip home from Nice with Don Ø in 2024. Featuring Anders Mielke and Mathias Sunekær Norsgaard. item.description: We’re going with a clickbait title. What happened, who did what, and how is he doing? There are many questions after Mads’ crash. Listen to this episode for the answers. item.description: Spencer Martin and Johan Bruyneel break down Remco Evenepoel’s dominant Volta Valenciana victory and what it says about the upcoming showdown with the sport’s biggest stars. They also discuss how Jonas Vingegaard’s coach abruptly leaving the team at the start of the season could affect the GC star, and what it means alongside Visma’s growing problems. They finish by talking about the news that rising Hollywood star Austin Butler will play Lance Armstrong in a biopic directed by Ed Berger and written by Zach Baylin, including who they think should play Johan. item.title: #29 Bak Lane: Kasper Asgreen
item.description: After a freezing four-hour ride in the hills around Vejle, Kasper Asgreen stops by Bak Lane at home in Kolding. In this episode, we set the classic Bak Lane stories aside for a while and instead dive into Asgreen’s season preparation: How has training gone? What has been the focus over the winter? And what goals and ambitions is he bringing into the coming season? An honest conversation about form, expectations, and spring. Host: Lars Bak Guest: Kasper Asgreen Bak Lane is produced by Anders Mielke / Forhjulslir.

item.title: 400: Jesper Mørkøv on Visma’s Season Start and a Preview of the UAE Tour
item.description: Unfortunately, we won’t get to see Jonas Vingegaard in his planned season debut at next week’s UAE Tour. Overall, it has been a somewhat unlucky start to the season for Visma Lease-a-Bike, who, after Simon Yates’ retirement, a cyclocross crash for Wout van Aert, and illness for both Vingegaard and Sepp Kuss, have had to pin their hopes on the phenomenon Matthew Brennan at the start of 2026. The team’s Danish sports director Jesper Mørkøv shares his thoughts in this episode of Veloropa Podcast on what kind of rider Brennan is and what Visma expects from him this season and beyond. One rider who has also started well and will be there on Monday when the UAE Tour begins is Remco Evenepoel. He is the clear favorite after six wins in four races in Spain. We look at the other contenders and at a route change that may affect the outcome in the United Arab Emirates. Veloropa Podcast is presented by Betano (betano.dk) and the electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk).

item.description: At last, there’s a race report from Volta a Comunitat Valenciana, where Matson made his debut in the Lidl-Trek armor. It was a successful start personally, but also a hard and difficult beginning, as the boss, Mads P, crashed and withdrew with broken bones. Hear Matson’s emotional account, plus recon rides for Milan-San Remo and Provence this weekend. Featuring Anders Mielke and Mathias Sunekær Norsgaard. item.description: Sarah Sturm is one of the most recognized and beloved names in gravel racing. She’s a powerhouse on the bike and a huge personality off it. In this interview, I got a behind-the-scenes look at her life as a professional athlete. We talk about how she trains, what she eats, and why she tracks her menstrual cycle. If you’re interested in the world of professional gravel racing, you won’t want to miss this episode. ⭐ Sponsored by Cycle Limited. item.title: S1 Ep683: The WorldTour Race Where the Pros Averaged Under 100W!
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we look at the surprisingly easy stages of the UAE Tour, where pro riders were averaging less than 100 watts. We also discuss a sobering new study on whether car drivers actually understand cyclist hand signals, and check in on Conor Dunne’s progress and eating habits at the Atlas Mountain Race. Plus, we reveal the most successful race bike in history and take a look at your latest ingenious hacks and questionable bodges. Chapters: ⏱️ 00:00 - Welcome to the GCN Show! 00:45 - Tadej Pogačar & Slim Shady 01:15 - Lance Armstrong Movie News 02:27 - Racing at 100 Watts: UAE Tour 13:51 - Cycling Shorts - Atlas Mountain Race Update 18:22 - Do Drivers Understand Cyclist Signals? 25:12 - The Most Successful Race Bike Ever? 29:49 - Hack or Bodge of the Week 36:26 - Caption Competition 39:27 Comment of the week DISCLAIMER: Conor has unfortunately had to pull out of the Atlas Mountain Race, but stay tuned for the video coming soon to see what happened!

item.title: Remco in the Breakaway and Mads P’s Crash
item.description: Forhjulslir is presented in collaboration with Continental Dæk Danmark. Continental is the most successful tire brand in the WorldTour peloton, from Tadej Pogačar in the rainbow jersey to Tobias Lund down under. Safety starts with the tire, both on the motorway and the bike path. The 2026 cycling season is only a few weeks old, but it is already full of drama, not least Danish drama. Jonas Vingegaard crashed in training and later became ill, which means he has to skip his season debut at the UAE Tour. Mads Pedersen opened his season in Valencia on Wednesday, but a heavy crash ended with a broken collarbone and wrist. What does that mean for Denmark’s biggest cycling stars as they head toward their major goals? It has not all been bad news, though. Tobias Lund has already taken two WorldTour wins Down Under in the new Decathlon colors, and Remco Evenepoel has started strongly with six wins in eight race days for Red Bull–Bora–Hansgrohe. Finally, we turn our attention to Visma-Lease a Bike, who no longer sit firmly on the throne - what is the discourse surrounding the yellow bees right now? Featuring Emil Mielke Vinjebo, Per Bausager, and Anders Mielke.

item.title: 2:2 Pogačar, the Performances, and the Doping Rumors
item.description: Tadej Pogačar has, in recent years, delivered performances that are levels above his rivals. In the previous episode of this feature, the focus was on the physiology behind those performances and why doping accusations and cycling are so closely linked. In this second and final episode, the focus is on cycling culture, followed by the debate around the performances and the journalistic framing and focus. Recording, editing, voice-over, and production: Frederik Muff. Guest contributors: John Jäger, Per Bausager, Ask Vest Christiansen, and Lars Nybo. Support the show.

item.title: 1:2 Pogačar, the Performances, and the Doping Rumors
item.description: Tadej Pogačar has, over several seasons, delivered performances that are several levels above his closest rivals. In Denmark especially, this has sparked heated debate about whether it is because the Slovenian is a once-in-a-century talent in the sport - or whether we should be especially critical given cycling’s past. In this two-part podcast, Frederik Muff explores the topic and lays the groundwork for an informed discussion. The first episode focuses especially on why cycling and doping accusations are so closely connected, and what we should pay attention to when looking at human performance. Recording, editing, voice-over, and production: Frederik Muff. Support the show.

item.title: 2:2 Doping: Pogačar, the Performances, and the Doping Rumors
item.description: Tadej Pogačar has, in recent years, delivered performances that are levels above his rivals. In the previous episode of this feature, the focus was on the physiology behind those performances and why doping accusations and cycling are so closely linked. In this second and final episode, the focus is on cycling culture, followed by the debate around the performances and the journalistic framing and focus. Recording, editing, voice-over, and production: Frederik Muff. Guest contributors: John Jäger, Per Bausager, Ask Vest Christiansen, and Lars Nybo. Support the show.

item.title: 1:2 Doping: Pogačar, the Performances, and the Doping Rumors
item.description: Tadej Pogačar has, over several seasons, delivered performances that are several levels above his closest rivals. In Denmark especially, this has sparked heated debate about whether it is because the Slovenian is a once-in-a-century talent in the sport - or whether we should be especially critical given cycling’s past. In this two-part podcast, Frederik Muff explores the topic and lays the groundwork for an informed discussion. item.title: Matthew Riccitello on Removing His Power Meter and Racing by Feel
item.description: Matthew Riccitello is one of the most exciting young climbers in the WorldTour. Fresh off a breakthrough fifth place at the Vuelta a España, the 23-year-old American joins Matt Stephens from altitude camp in Sierra Nevada to reflect on his journey so far and his move to Decathlon CMA CGM Team. In this episode, Matthew discusses GC ambitions, training philosophy, racing without a power meter, and why consistency and confidence are his greatest strengths. Matt also learns about life in his hometown of Tucson and his adopted European home of Nice. Wise beyond his years, Riccitello explains why he feels the final week of a Grand Tour is the best stage on which to shine. www.sigmasports.com Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices

item.title: 399: Mads P’s Spring in Danger? Another Win for Tobias Lund
item.description: Mads P’s season got off to almost the worst possible start when he crashed on stage 1 of his season opener in Valenciana and broke both his collarbone and wrist. His planned spring preparations may now be thrown in the bin, and cycling Denmark is waiting for news on how serious it is for the popular former world champion. We talk about the possible consequences for the season and what a plan B might be in the worst case. It is hard to say that Tobias Lund Andresen has had a bad start to the season. He has already taken two WorldTour wins in Australia, and you can hear from the fast Dane in this episode of Veloropa Podcast, where we also celebrate the Danish gold medal winners at the European Track Cycling Championships and discuss Remco Evenepoel’s dominant start in Spain for his new team Bora-Hansgrohe. Veloropa Podcast is presented in collaboration with meal kits from HelloFresh (hellofresh.dk) and the electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk).

item.title: Mads P’s Spring in Danger? Another Win for Tobias Lund
150. Brian Smith: National Champion, Racing with Lance, Saying No to EPO, and Snakes in Showers
item.description: This week, James and Will are joined by Scottish ex-pro turned team manager, commentator, and pundit Brian Smith. Brian cut his teeth in the 1980s at the school of cycling hard knocks, ACBB - Athletic Club de Boulogne-Billancourt - in Paris, whose alumni include Jacques Anquetil, Stephen Roche, and Pippa York, before turning pro with the legendary British Banana team in 1991. Three successful seasons followed, including his first British national road race title, which led to Brian catching the eye of the Motorola team bosses. He signed for the American outfit in 1994 and went on to race alongside Lance Armstrong, Andy Hampsten, and Phil Anderson. Brian won his second British national road race title that same year, but as we discuss in our chat, despite such success he found himself disillusioned and on the outside of a sport he loved as cycling became embroiled in its darkest doping years - something Brian wanted no part of. He hung up his racing wheels in 1996 but returned to the pro peloton in 2011 as directeur sportif for Endura Racing, where he stayed as it evolved into MTN-Qhubeka and then Dimension Data, before retiring in 2014. Today, Brian can be found with a microphone in hand as a commentator and pundit for Eurosport, and among other parts of that job, he discusses his love of commentating and watching the lesser-known Tour of Hellas, a five-stage race around Greece taking place from 6th to 10th May this year, so keep an eye out for that one. There are probably few people with as much experience across the last four decades of cycling as Brian, and certainly few people who talk so candidly about everything from being invited into 'the program' by Lance Armstrong to having a snake thrown into his shower by Shane Sutton. So get the kettle, pin back your lugholes, and listen to this one. Interview begins at 3.44 ------------------ The Tour of Hellas in Greece is a five-stage race taking place from 6th - 10th May 2026. Find out more here, and check with wherever you watch your cycling for broadcast times ------------------ This episode is brought to you by the Hammerhead Karoo GPS bike computer. item.title: S1 Ep682: Have We Reached Peak Wheel-Sucking?
item.description: In this week’s GCN Show, we discuss the controversial training crash involving Jonas Vingegaard and the heated debate over wheel-sucking etiquette on public roads. We also celebrate Mathieu van der Poel’s historic eighth world title and look at the major Strava cleanup that deleted millions of incorrectly categorized activities. Plus, we cover the latest e-bike legislation and Google’s new AI navigation features for cyclists.

item.title: #90 Gruppetto on Forhjulslir: The Cow Is Going Out to Pasture
item.description: It’s finally happening. The cow is going out to pasture for the first time in 2026. Tomorrow, Tuesday, Matson is ready for his debut race in a Lidl-Trek kit, alongside Mads Pedersen, against riders including Remco Evenepoel, João Almeida, and Biniam Girmay. We look ahead to the season opener and dig into all the thoughts, expectations, and nerves that build up when the first race numbers go on the skinsuit. Featuring: Anders Mielke and Mathias Sunekær Norsgaard. Gruppetto on Forhjulslir is sponsored by Aioss. item.title: Søjberg’s French February Mission
item.description: Forhjulslir is presented in collaboration with Continental Dæk Danmark. Continental is the most successful tire in the WorldTour peloton, from Tadej Pogačar in the rainbow jersey to Tobias Lund down under. Safety starts with the tire, both on the highway and on the bike path. Rasmus Søjberg joins Forhjulslir just before he boards a flight to the season opener in the French stage race Étoile de Bessèges. He dreams of a stage win and the feeling of crossing the line first early in the season. We also hear about his preparation for 2026, after a training camp with Decathlon CMA-CGM and sharing a room with the team’s new Classics signing, Tiesj Benoot. What kind of music does Benoot listen to? And what has it been like for Søjberg to become part of the team’s new sprint group with Olav Kooij, Tobias Lund, and sports director Mark Renshaw? We dig into all that and more on today’s stage. Guest: Rasmus Søjberg Pedersen. Host: Anders Mielke.

item.title: Radio Tour – in Spain: On Training Camp with Mads P., Skjelmose, and Norsgaard
item.description: Anders Mielke has gathered the whole Lidl-Trek crew in Spain. Mathias Norsgaard, Mattias Skjelmose, and Mads P. share good grind and their outlook for a big new cycling year with the three Danes in the Lidl-Trek jersey.

item.title: Radio Tour – in the Breakaway: On Training Camp with Mads P., Skjelmose, and Norsgaard
item.description: Anders Mielke has gathered the whole Lidl-Trek crew in Spain. Mathias Norsgaard, Mattias Skjelmose, and Mads P. share good grind and their outlook for a big new cycling year with the three Danes in the Lidl-Trek jersey.

item.title: Cold or Warm Banana?
item.description: It doesn’t stop with the bananas. We’re back with banana news, the kangaroo scandal, more team time trial talk, this week’s blunder in Saudi, and the year’s style points for Julius. We follow up on Camilla, planning for this year’s Christmas party has already begun, and there are a few stories from our bachelor party(ies). That and much more in this episode of Lang Distance.

item.title: 398: What We’re Looking Forward to in 2026
item.description: Jonas Vingegaard is set to ride both the Giro and the Tour, but he crashed on a training ride in Málaga today. It was worse for Mikkel Bjerg, who was rammed by a kangaroo in Australia. But which Danes are we most excited to see in 2026? Will Albert Philipsen have his international breakthrough? Will Mads P win a Monument? Will Tobias Lund take his first Grand Tour victory? And what about Lidl-Trek and Bora, who have strengthened significantly ahead of this season with a wealth of GC riders, while QuickStep returns to the Classics. item.title: S1 Ep681: The Cycling Industry Has Officially Gone Nuts!
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we debate whether the cycling industry has officially lost its mind with the release of €950 3D-printed sunglasses and the growing trend of premiumisation. We also look at the controversial new legislation in New Jersey that could require e-bike riders to have licenses and insurance, plus a revolutionary e-bike with a 1,000 km range. Finally, we round up the racing action, from Mathieu van der Poel’s historic record-breaking weekend to a literal kangaroo causing chaos in the Tour Down Under peloton.

item.title: #89 Gruppettoen on Forhjulslir: Roubaix Recon
item.description: The pace stays high, and there are now only 76 days until Paris-Roubaix. Hear about Lidl-Trek’s recent recon rides for Roubaix, Flanders, and E3. Many listeners have asked about the length of Matson’s stem — and of course, you’ll get the answer. Plus a solid batch of anecdotes from a week that included night work, a kangaroo collarbone, and the legendary allergy hotel. Featuring: Anders Mielke & Mathias Sunekær Norsgaard. item.title: The Blue Nail and Camilla
item.description: Just when we thought we could walk on water, the internet connection drops. Huge apologies. But that’s just how we are. For the worried: don’t worry, Mads has had a bandage put on his hand. We deep-dive into the preparations for this year’s classics, the Danes Down Under this week, The Blue Nail and Camilla — which could sound like the title of something rather naughty, but that’s not the kind of show we are — the banana cake gate, the sound of sea salt and cola, and much more in this episode of Lang Distance.

item.title: Alistair Brownlee on Life After Gold Medals, Gravel Racing, and His Yorkshire Roots
item.description: Double Olympic gold medallist Alistair Brownlee joins Matt Stephens from his home in Yorkshire for a wide-ranging conversation about life after elite triathlon. Brownlee reflects on retiring from professional sport, the pressure of performing at a home Olympics, and why daily movement still matters as much as ever. The pair dive into Alistair’s growing love of gravel racing, sibling rivalry with brother Jonathan, and that unforgettable moment at the 2016 World Triathlon Series. There’s also time for business ventures, community impact, and a proper Yorkshire Bramhope quiz covering pubs, tunnels, chippies, and baked goods. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices

item.title: #28 Bak Lane: Michael Mørkøv
item.description: There’s a special guest in Bak Lane when the sport’s newest Hall of Fame member and Denmark’s national coach, Michael Mørkøv, joins the microphone. With a CV packed with world championships, Olympic titles, international medals, and iconic lead-out wins for some of the world’s fastest sprinters, Mørkøv is one of Danish cycling’s biggest names. And luckily, I got to know him up close myself: I was teammates with Mørkøv in 2009 on Team Saxo Bank and have raced with and against him in the WorldTour peloton for 10 seasons. Join Bak Lane and hear Mørkøv talk about some of his biggest experiences — both on the road and on the track. Produced by Anders Mielke / Forhjulslir

item.title: 397: Victory for Tobias Lund and Off-Season Bombshells
item.description: The first Danish victory of the year, and therefore also the first Veloropa Podcast of the year. There is no Veloropa Podcast without a Danish win. And it came quickly in 2026, when Tobias Lund Andresen opened Denmark’s win account on stage 1 of the Tour Down Under, took his first WorldTour victory, and rode into the race leader’s jersey (for a brief moment...). In addition to Tobias Lund’s fine win, this episode looks at the biggest headlines of the off-season, not least Simon Yates’ sudden retirement and Jonas Vingegaard’s Giro focus. We also dropped our own little bombshell: we’re planning to launch on YouTube in 2026. Veloropa Podcast is presented in collaboration with electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk) and the Danish supplement brand Aioss (use discount code veloropa at aioss.dk and get DKK 300 off).

item.title: Victory for Tobias Lund and Off-Season Bombshells
149. The Legend of Eddy Merckx, with Lee Donaldson
item.description: This week, James is joined by Lee Donaldson, filmmaker and passionate cycling fan, to discuss his upcoming documentary 525: The Unstoppable Eddy Merckx. Lee describes 525 as having “the greatest cast of racing champions ever seen in a cycling documentary,” and it’s hard to disagree. Contributors include Mark Cavendish, Bradley Wiggins, Lotte Kopecky, Bernard Hinault, Sean Kelly, Roger De Vlaeminck, fashion designer Paul Smith, and Merckx’s old mechanic, Ernesto Colnago, plus Eddy Merckx’s family and, of course, the man himself. As you might have guessed, Lee now has an encyclopedic knowledge of The Cannibal to rival his personal passion, so plug in, sit back, and enjoy our Merckx-centric chat, which covers everything from Merckx’s insane Grand Tour performances to his tragic crash in Blois, to what it was like going to lunch with the main man. Interview begins at 3.44 ------------------ 525: The Unstoppable Eddy Merckx is produced by Lee Donaldson and Simon Hardy’s 6 Day Films. Find out more, including where you can watch it, here. ------------------ This episode is brought to you by the Hammerhead Karoo GPS bike computer.
