item.title: NEVER STRAYS RAJ, PART 2: POINTY STICKS, BROKEN TRACKS AND NICK OWEN
item.description: Ned guesses at birdlife, visits a semi-derelict velodrome, and reminisces about the end of free-to-air Tour coverage. Register your support for NSF Live In France... every sign-up helps! Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices

item.title: S1 Ep680: Is This What We Really Want?
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we take a deep dive into Van Rysel’s outrageous FTP2 concept bike and ask whether it really is a glimpse into the future of performance cycling. We also explore the brand-new Pinarello Grevil MX, a gravel bike designed to bridge the gap between road speed and mountain bike capability. Plus, we cover record-breaking ultra-endurance news, controversial rule changes in US gravel racing, and the release of our long-awaited Peru adventure film.

item.title: #88 Gruppettoen on Forhjulslir: January Streak 🔥
item.description: The New Year’s resolution continues, and Gruppettoen’s podcast feed is as hot as the gyms in January. Since last time, Matson has been on a date away from the hotel to do technique training with Juan Ayuso and Derek Gee, and got a ride of epic proportions from Mads P, Jonathan Milan, and Mathias Vacek. The final anecdotes from the Dénia camp are sprinkled through this edition of Gruppettoen on Forhjulslir. Featuring: Anders Mielke & Mathias Sunekær Norsgaard.

item.title: Never Strays Raj: A Cycling Journey to India
item.description: Ned is in India for the Pune Grand Tour, part one. Register your support for NSF Live in France — every sign-up helps! Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices

item.title: I’m Stepping From One Shit Box Into Another
item.description: We’re keeping the pace up and back with the second episode of the year. What better way to kick things off than with two bottles of snaps from DJ Preben? We also stop by Lidl-Trek and a driving skills track, this week’s home is in Middelfart, Jakob is training for baby swimming, there’s this month’s Christmas calendar, and remember to wear a good bike helmet before you get run over by a Dacia. MORE MERCH in the spring — the DM is open for ideas and requests.

item.title: Meta Vanguard: The Big Review of Oakley’s New AI Glasses
item.description: A pair of cycling glasses you can talk to, that record video of your ride and play your favorite music. That may sound like something from the distant future, but it’s already possible with Oakley’s new sports glasses, Meta Vanguard. Frederik Muff and the podcast host received two pairs of the cycling glasses from Profil Optik (which we’re giving away as prizes among supporters on 10er.dk), and we’ve tested them for a good month to provide a thorough and objective review. Listen in and hear whether they should be your next cycling gadget — or whether you should keep riding au natural. Support the show

item.title: Radio Tour in Spain: Vingegaard’s Big Giro Ambition
item.description: Anders Mielke has traveled to Spain for a press day with Team Visma Lease a Bike, where Jonas Vingegaard reveals his season plans. Also hear from teammates Matteo Jorgenson and Victor Campenaerts, Head of Racing Grischa Niermann, and Head of Performance Mathieu Heijboer.

item.title: Never Strays Frozen: Penguins and Postboxes
item.description: Ned meets NSF’s marketing manager near the South Pole. Register your support for NSF Live in France — every sign-up helps! Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices

item.title: S1 Ep679: Are Cyclists Burning Out Earlier Than Ever?
item.description: This week on The GCN Show, we dive into the shocking news of Simon Yates’ retirement and ask whether the extreme intensity of the modern pro era is causing riders to burn out sooner than ever before. We look at how training, diet, and pressure have changed the peloton since 2020. Plus, we cover a huge list of retirements across the industry, a bizarre 500 km ride through a McDonald’s drive-thru, and a heated copycat controversy in aero tech. Win a @Vision_Tech_Usa Metron 5D handlebar: https://gcn.eu/GCNVisionCompetitionJan 💥

item.title: #87 The Gruppetto on Forhjulslir: Live from Spain
item.description: The Gruppetto is finally together again — in person, face to face — with full focus and plenty of energy. We recorded the episode in Dénia, where Lidl-Trek is holding its January camp. Matson shares more wild stories from training with Mads P in Nice, and we’ve calculated that he has eaten more than 200 eggs in just 20 days. Mielke is also feeling strong after a couple of thrilling rides in Calpe, but is also looking forward to work and the season starting now. Featuring Anders Mielke and Mathias Sunekær Norsgaard.

item.title: Car Podcast of the Year
item.description: Car Podcast of the Year (thanks to Mandsholdet) returns after a tough period for Mads and Jakob. Jakob has just become a father and Mads has turned 30. In this episode, we כמובן cover those two events and the major investment Jakob has had to make because of them. We give our weekly recommendation, the worst Christmas in 27 years, the best and worst WorldTour jerseys of 2026, whether bananas belong in layer cake, whether Gatorade is really a cheap hack drink, a balance bike on Arnolds Knold, and much more.

item.title: Julian Alaphilippe on Racing with Instinct, Fatherhood, and Finding Balance at Tudor
item.description: Former world champion Julian Alaphilippe joins Matt Stephens from Tudor Pro Cycling’s training camp near Calpe to reflect on a career defined by courage, instinct, and unforgettable moments. Julian opens up about leading the Tour de France, the pressure of wearing the rainbow jersey, and why that period was both a peak and a personal challenge. He discusses becoming a father, finding balance beyond results, and why joining Tudor has reignited his motivation. The conversation also explores modern cycling’s obsession with numbers versus feel, the responsibility of leadership, and the emotions behind his standout victory in Québec, before a lively Saint-Amand-Montrond hometown quiz rounds things off. www.sigmasports.com Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices

item.title: 148. Hunter Allen and the Future of Training and Racing
item.description: This week James, and Will at the beginning, is joined by Hunter Allen, co-founder of TrainingPeaks, longtime elite-level cycling coach to pros and US national teams, and the man who literally wrote the book on power meters back when torque measurement was just a twinkle in an SRM engineer’s eye. That book, fittingly enough, was called Training + Racing With a Power Meter, and it put Hunter at the pioneering frontier of what would become the gold standard in cycling metrics: watts. Last year Hunter returned with his new book Training + Competing With a Continuous Glucose Monitor, and in this episode we discuss why continuous glucose monitors, or CGMs, are set to become the new power meter and how they and the sports science behind them are already contributing to the rise in speeds in the pro peloton. In short, CGMs can help a rider and coach work out exactly when to fuel, and how much, for maximum performance, and that means Pogačar and company are breaking more records. Hunter also talks about how to understand training data, the relationship between heart rate and power, managing training load and fatigue, and how to develop better nutrition strategies to ride faster. It’s a get-the-kettle-on kind of podcast, and maybe fetch a pen and paper too, because Hunter knows his stuff and goes pretty deep. Find out more about Training + Competing With a Continuous Glucose Monitor here. Interview begins at 9:59. This episode is brought to you by the Hammerhead Karoo GPS bike computer. item.title: #27 Bak Lane: Mads Würtz Schmidt
item.description: Welcome to a new season of Bak Lane. The first episode of the year features my good friend Mads Würtz Schmidt, with whom I raced for a couple of seasons in the World Tour peloton. Since then, I have retired and Würtz has turned his attention to gravel roads. In this episode of Bak Lane, we hear about Würtz’s gravel adventure, where he became European champion last year and has just signed a new and exciting deal with Specialized, which is investing heavily in the rapidly growing gravel scene. Host: Lars Bak. Produced by Anders Mielke / Forhjulslir.

item.title: #86 Gruppettoen on Forhjulslir: Locked In
item.description: Welcome to a new season of Gruppettoen on Forhjulslir, 2026 edition. We promised a higher frequency in the new year - here you go. Matson reports from the training camp regime in Nice, but is actually more curious to hear about Mielke’s training holiday and café stops in Calpe. Guests: Anders Mielke & Mathias Sunekær Norsgaard.

item.title: S1 Ep678: Lost Fitness Over Christmas? Do This
item.description: Welcome back to the GCN Show! This week, we share six essential tips to help you get back on track after the festive break and stay consistent throughout the new year. We also cover the latest cycling news, including Bosch’s new anti-theft measures, updated 2026 pro team kits, and the surprising laws surrounding drinking and cycling.

item.title: The Big Gravel Tire Test
item.description: In gravel riding, tire choice matters even more than on the road. There are big differences in everything from tread patterns to rubber compounds, and it can be hard for everyday riders to choose the right tire. In this episode, two of Denmark’s best gravel racers and an experienced recreational rider test five of the most popular gravel tires right now: Continental Terra Speed, Schwalbe G-One RS Pro, Hutchinson Caracal Race, Challenge Strada Biancha, and Specialized Pathfinder. We test everything from lap times on a circuit to braking distances. Most importantly, you’ll get an explanation of what each tire is especially good at. Support the show.

item.title: The Big 2026 Transfer Review
item.description: You’re tuned in to a new season of Forhjulslir, 2026 edition, once again presented by World Tour’s most successful tire from Continental. A good third of the World Tour peloton will ride Continental tires in 2026 - including, of course, last year’s best cyclist in the world, Tadej Pogacar. Safety starts with the tire. Happy New Year! We’re almost there. The 2026 cycling season. In just 20 days, the first World Tour stage race, Tour Down Under, begins, and shortly after that the European roads wake up again. It is January 1, 2026. A new season is upon us, and with it the changes we need to get used to: new riders in new jerseys, new talents on new bikes, and of course the riders themselves - today we focus on the men’s riders and men’s teams. We go through all 18 World Tour teams and the best Pro Tour teams we can find in the 2026 cycling peloton - some are new, some have changed names, and others have changed DNA and identity. We look at the new rider arrivals on each team and discuss what expectations we have for each team and its riders. And naturally, the Danish riders too, with a total of 28 Danes on the men’s World Tour in 2026. With that, happy New Year, merry Christmas and welcome to season 8 of Forhjulslir and this year’s first stage: “The Big 2026 Transfer Review.” Guests: Per Bausager, Emil Mielke Vinjebo & Anders Mielke.

item.title: S1 Ep677: 6 Things You Need To Know About Cycling In 2026
item.description: Welcome to the final GCN Show of the year, where Si and Dan dive into the trends that will shape the cycling world in 2026! We tackle the big debate: has Tadej Pogačar officially surpassed Eddy Merckx as the greatest male cyclist in history? From the rise of "gromestiques" and 32-inch mountain bike wheels to the shifting science of Zone 3 training, we’re looking ahead at what’s next for the sport. Plus, we look back at our epic adventures from the past year and feature your most ingenious (and questionable) hacks and bodges.

item.title: #26 Bak Lane: Jens Voigt
item.description: Bak Lane wraps up its first season with a Berlin special. I’ve headed to the German capital to visit my old teammate Jens Voigt, with whom I raced for Team CSC/Saxo Bank for five seasons from 2005 to 2009. Join Bak Lane for stories from Berlin, BS’s survival trips, Voigt’s successful career, and the glorious CSC days. Thanks for listening in 2025. See you again - Bak Lane - in 2026. Host: Lars Bak. This episode is in English. Produced by Anders Mielke / Forhjulslir.

item.title: #85 Gruppettoen on Forhjulslir: Happy New Year!
item.description: Gruppettoen wishes all our dear listeners a happy and safe New Year with one last episode in 2025. Matson shares training anecdotes from Calpe with Mads P and Vacek, as well as from Nice, where he’ll celebrate New Year with heat training and the taste of blood in his mouth. Thanks for listening in 2025 - see you again in 2026 #morebookkeepinginbed Guests: Anders Mielke & Mathias Sunekær Norsgaard. Gruppettoen on Forhjulslir is sponsored by Aioss. By buying Aioss, you’re not just supporting us - but most importantly yourself, with more physical and mental energy in everyday life. Use the code “gruppettoen” and get 100 DKK off the first 3 months. Read more at: https://aioss.dk/pages/grupettoen

item.title: 147. David Millar: The Christmas Special (Sort Of)
item.description: In this week’s episode, Will and James are joined by David Millar, a man who needs no introduction, but just in case: David’s career in cycling spans decorated rider (he was the first Brit to wear all three Grand Tour jerseys) to fallen hero (he admitted to, and was suspended for two years for doping in 2004) to risen phoenix (he returned to the pro peloton as an outspoken 'clean' rider and raced successfully until 2014) to writer, broadcaster and commentator (up until this summer he was 'the voice' of ITV cycling along with co-commentator Ned Boulting) to business owner (with clothing brand Chpt III) to brand director at Factor bikes (finally nothing more to write in brackets). Here, David chats about turning pro back in the 1990s and those difficult earlier years; the devastation of ITV losing its TV coverage and him his job; his new life at Factor bikes and the science behind Factor's new superbike, the Factor One; Factor parting ways with Israel-Premier Tech for political reasons; what it's like having your sister, Fran, as CEO of Rapha; whether he's duty-bound to ride the Rapha Festive 500; and his general and utter love for cycling. Merry Christmas, and thanks for listening for a whole year (or just reading this bit, possibly never having listened at all). Interview begins at 11.34 This episode is brought to you by the Hammerhead Karoo GPS bike computer. item.title: S1 Ep676: Tadej Pogačar Just Got Even Better: Everybody Should Be Scared
item.description: Welcome to a special Christmas edition of the GCN Show, where we analyze Tadej Pogačar’s "scary" early-season form after he obliterated the famous Coll de Rates KOM during a massive training ride in Spain. We also take a look at the bold new 2026 kit reveals from Ineos Grenadiers and Canyon-SRAM, plus the news of a controversial billion-dollar bike path project. Finally, we celebrate the festive season with a special wheel-themed edition of Hack or Bodge and round up the latest ultra-cycling world records. Donate 👉 https://www.hernehillvelodrome.com/festive500

item.title: Christmas Secrets
item.description: Listen to the full episode on Podimo.dk/eddy. The Christmas spirit has settled heavily over Café Eddy when Edie Choleva and Jesper Mørkøv visit the café. Edie has brought strong glögg and homemade baked goods, while Jesper has been in Calbe on a training camp. The latest news from the cycling world is discussed, Andreas Lydmand brings surprises, and Brian is carrying a Christmas secret he refuses to share with anyone. Now Christmas can finally begin. Hosts: Brian Holm and Bastian Emil. Guests: Edie Choleva and Jesper Mørkøv. Producer: Andreas 'Lydmand' Saxild. Editor: Sarah Ørsted. Listen to Café Eddy exclusively on Podimo. Get a great offer at podimo.dk/eddy. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: The World No. 1: Meet Noah "Tarzan" Lindholm
item.description: Forhjulslir is presented in collaboration with Continental Dæk Danmark. Remember to drive with winter tires when the temperature is below seven degrees. Continental offers award-winning winter and all-season tires. Safety starts with the tire - on both the bike and the car. "Who is this Noah Andersen?" That is the question I have been asked again and again by foreign cycling journalists during this year's Grand Tours. And for good reason. 18-year-old Noah "Tarzan" Lindholm Møller Andersen from Trorød has taken the cycling world by storm. He ends 2025 as number 1 on all world rankings for U19 riders and has drawn attention from both Danish and international eyes. The next step is already in place: in 2026 he will move from Red Bull - Bora - Hansgrohe's junior team, Team Grenke Auto Eder, up to their U23 squad. But who is he really, the kid from Trorød? In this major interview, we get up close to the world number one. Hear the wild story of how, as a child, he was offered a place at Chelsea's football academy but chose cycling and the open road instead. We dive into a cycling-mad family, where his mother Bettina followed uncle Rolf Sørensen's career closely, and talk about the bold decision to leave school and bet everything on his cycling dream at just 16. Host: Anders Mielke

item.title: #25 Bak Lane: André Greipel 2/2
item.description: I traveled to Germany, near Rostock, to visit my former teammate, good friend, and one of the best sprinters in cycling history, André Greipel. Over two longer recordings, we really get time to catch up: how we are doing today, how Greipel experienced retiring, and we share a number of memories while diving into the highlights and reflections from his remarkable career. Host: Lars Bak. The episode is in English. This is part two of two recordings. The first part of the conversation with André Greipel was released on Thursday, December 18. Produced by Anders Mielke / Forhjulslir.

item.title: Andrew Strohmeyer on Chasing the European Cyclocross Dream
item.description: 2025 Pan American Cyclocross Champion Andrew Strohmeyer joins Matt Stephens from his European base in the Netherlands to discuss racing World Cups, funding a full European season, and chasing a professional career. A multiple US national champion and Pan-American title holder, Andrew explains the "Mud Fund," the impact of gravel's boom in the US, and the technical skill gap between American and European racing. He breaks down his impressive 12th place at the Tábor World Cup, the demands of elite cyclocross training, and why the discipline keeps pushing him to improve - before contemplating the existence of exploding goats in the Mount Airy, Maryland hometown quiz. www.sigmasports.com Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices

item.title: #25 Bak Lane: André Greipel 1/2
item.description: I traveled to Germany, near Rostock, to visit my former teammate, good friend, and one of the best sprinters in cycling history, André Greipel. Over two longer recordings, we really get time to catch up: how we are doing today, how Greipel experienced retiring, and we share a number of memories while diving into the highlights and reflections from his remarkable career. Host: Lars Bak. The episode is in English. This is part one of two recordings. Produced by Anders Mielke / Forhjulslir.

item.title: How Ben Healy Became the Master of the Breakaway
item.description: Ben Healy has quickly established himself as one of the most exciting riders in the pro peloton, known for his relentless aggression and long-range attacks. We sat down with the EF Education-EasyPost star to discuss exactly how he masters the art of the breakaway, from his "almost perfect" stage wins to his obsession with aerodynamics. Ben also reveals the huge fueling numbers needed to sustain his attacks and how he uses fatigue resistance to wear down his rivals.

item.title: S1 Ep675: Does Cycling Have to Be Dangerous?
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we weigh up the risks of riding and ask: does cycling always have to be dangerous? We also take a first look at the new 2026 World Tour team kits, break down Tadej Pogačar's surprisingly light race schedule ahead of the Tour, and catch up on Mathieu van der Poel's return to cyclocross. Plus, all your usual favorites, including Hack or Bodge and a fresh Caption Competition.

item.title: Lidl Trek Camp with the Intern
item.description: We sent a microphone with our sound engineer and intern Kristian Egholm to Lidl Trek's December training camp. There is everything from financial advice to ordering the cake at the airport, what you always carry in your hand luggage, winter swimming in the outdoor pool, and a late weigh-in with the doctor. Featuring: Kristian Egholm (host), Martin Pedersen, Patrick Frydkjær, Tim Teutenberg, Søren Kragh Andersen, Jakob Söderkvist, Mads Pedersen, Sebastian Andersen, and Mathias Norsgaard.

item.title: A Look into the Danish World Tour Crystal Ball #2: The Classics and Grand Tour Team
item.description: In this second episode about the Danish World Tour riders' 2026 season, the two experts Christian Moberg Jørgensen and Mathias Mundbjerg once again take us into the future. How will the Danish stars perform in 2026 - and, not least, which races will Danish riders win? The two experts also put together two squads for the fictional "Team Bagerstop," first creating an all-Danish Classics team and then an all-Danish Grand Tour team. Support the show.

item.title: 396: The Big Veloropa Awards Show
item.description: As tradition dictates, we are naming the year's best performances internationally and among the Danish riders. In front of an enthusiastic live audience of more than 100 loyal 10'er supporters at Service Course in Kokkedal, it was revealed who received the extremely prestigious awards in 2025. And the quiz was decided by the narrowest of margins. Veloropa Podcast is presented by the Danish supplement Aioss (aioss.dk), meal kits from HelloFresh (hellofresh.dk), and the electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk).

item.title: The Great Veloropa Award Show
146. Dr Hutch: Tears, resilience, beauty, and endurance
item.description: This week, James and Will are joined by Dr Hutch, the legendary British time-trial rider, second only to Beryl Burton in national wins, who became a Cambridge-educated doctor, then a journalist. Dr Hutch is a long-time columnist for Cycling Weekly (other cycling magazines are available; see our link to Cyclist below), commentator, pundit, voice of cycling authority, and author of several books, including the critically acclaimed The Hour, a candid account of his ill-fated attempt at the Hour record, Faster: The Obsession, Science and Luck Behind the World's Fastest Cyclists, and most recently Further: Seeking the Distant Limits of Cycling Endurance (Atlantic). In this episode, Dr Hutch explains the moment he 'cried for seven hours on a bike', how Haribo jelly snakes saved him, the mental madness and fortitude of endurance racing from national 24-hour time trials to the Transcon, what makes an endurance specialist special, and why failing to take the Hour was exactly what it says: a failure, but one that gave him lifelong lessons that have shaped his cycling ever since. The interview begins at 9:26. This episode is brought to you by the Hammerhead Karoo GPS bike computer. Visit hammerhead.io and use the code CYCLIST to get a free HR strap with every purchase (just be sure to add the strap to your cart, then apply the code at checkout). And it's also a rather lovely website about everything road cycling and gravel. Check us out at cyclist.co.uk. Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: #24 Bak Lane: Michael Valgren
item.description: In a new format on Bak Lane, my good friend Michael Valgren joins the podcast for a current conversation about what is happening in cycling right now. Valgren reveals a little about his winter training, teases a new fast Cannondale frame, and shares his goals and race program for 2026. We will try to catch up with each other during the 2026 season, so please send in your ideas for the format. Maybe you can do better than 'Valle and Volmer' and 'Bak Lane with the pink panther from Thy'. Host: Lars Bak Guest: Michael Valgren Bak Lane is produced by Anders Mielke / Forhjulslir.

item.title: S1 Ep674: The Mistake Most of Us Make When Training
item.description: In this week's GCN Show, Si and Dan discuss the dreaded 'Grey Zone' and why doing too much riding at this middle intensity might be making you tired without actually making you faster. We also look at a new study linking the world's happiest countries to cycling, and discuss the controversial new documentary narrated by Lance Armstrong. Plus, stay tuned for a massive counterfeit bike bust, a strange new electric vehicle from Honda, and your Hacks and Bodges!

item.title: The Literary Corner
item.description: Listen to the full episode on Podimo.dk/eddy. Author, journalist, and boxing writer Jonas Nyrup visits Café Eddy. The week that has passed is discussed: What about cyclists on TikTok? What about the women's Paris-Roubaix on the same day as the men's? What about driving licenses for young riders so they can handle themselves in the peloton? The podcast also turns to cycling's literary connection. How do you write a book? What is the best part of writing? Maitre Jacques has to respond to emails and wish lists, and Bastian Emil has the chapeau of his life - life at Café Eddy goes on quietly in the middle of December. Hosts: Brian Holm and Bastian Emil Guest: Jonas Nyrup Producer: Andreas 'Lydmand' Saxild Editor: Sarah Ørsted Listen to Café Eddy exclusively on Podimo. Get a good offer at podimo.dk/eddy Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: We’ve brought in a new handyman
item.description: You probably know him best for his financial advice, 'screw work and save on toilet paper' - of course, that is Lang Distance's super domestique and handyman Mathias Larsen, who joins Jakob in this episode. Sit back and see whether he pulls more gems out of the bag in this week's postpartum episode of Lang Distance. Note: the episode contains large amounts of Klovn quotes.

item.title: A look into the Danish World Tour crystal ball: Expectations and predictions for 2026
item.description: Danish cyclists are taking over the World Tour to an extent never seen before. At the same time, Danish riders hold second and fourth place in the world rankings. But what expectations do the experts have for the coming World Tour season? Will Vingegaard complete a Grand Tour hat-trick, and can Mads Pedersen get the Monument he wants? Listen here as Christian Moberg Jørgensen and Mathias Mundbjerg take a look into the crystal ball. Support the show.

item.title: 395: The Big Transfer Overview
item.description: It is time for this year's transfer overview. How have the individual teams strengthened themselves ahead of the new season? And which teams will need to get creative to withstand the loss of key riders? We also have an interview with Michael Blaudzun, who will become sports director at the new World Tour team Uno-X from next season. And as always, there is dramatic development in the quiz... Veloropa Podcast is presented by the Danish dietary supplement Aioss (aioss.dk, use the code velotest), meal kits from HelloFresh (hellofresh.dk), and the electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk).

item.title: The Big Transfer Overview
#23 Bak Lane: Kim Andersen
item.description: In today's Bak Lane, Lars Bak is joined by a true legend of Danish cycling: Kim Andersen. The man who, on July 4, 1983, wrote himself into the history books as the first Dane ever to wear the yellow jersey in the Tour de France. He is known for his crazy long breakaways, raw strength, and endless training rides, which made him an icon on the road. Kim was Lars' first sports director as a professional, and for more than 28 years he has shaped the peloton from the front seat of the sports director's car. The plan was for this year's Tour of Luxembourg to be his last race in the Lidl-Trek car, but retirement has been put on hold. Why, and what comes next, he explains in more detail. They also talk about Lidl-Trek's strong 2025 season and the Danish stars on the team: Mads P, Mattias Skjelmose, Søren Kragh Andersen, and Albert Withen Philipsen. Sit back and join Bak Lane. Host: Lars Bak. Produced by Anders Mielke & Forhjulslir.

item.title: S1 Ep673: We Can’t Believe We Used to Use These Products
item.description: Welcome to GCN Show 673. This week, we take a nostalgic trip through cycling products we once happily rode with but would never want to use now. From ancient gearing to questionable accessories, we look at how far cycling technology has come, alongside big news from pro cycling, urban cycling, and more.

item.title: A Philosophical Approach
item.description: Listen to the full episode on Podimo.dk/eddy. Sports director Sebastian Andersen visits Café Eddy and talks about life at Lidl-Trek and what it is like to be the most highly educated sports director in the peloton. What is the best part of working in the team car, and what is it like to follow in the footsteps of Kim Andersen? Marginal gains are discussed, and everyone takes a moment to reflect on their most beautiful moment in cycling. Hosts: Brian Holm and Bastian Emil. Guest: Sebastian Andersen. Producer: Andreas 'Lydmand' Saxild. Editor: Sarah Ørsted. Listen to Café Eddy exclusively on Podimo. Get a good offer at podimo.dk/eddy. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Papa P as Maternity Leave Substitute
item.description: Jakob has gone on paternity leave, so we have called in reinforcements in the form of Papa P. If you are curious about what a phone conversation between Mads and Claus might sound like, listen to this special episode of Lang Distance.

item.title: João Almeida on Grand Tours, Team Dynamics, and That Pidcock Moment
item.description: Portugal’s finest export since the pastel de nata, João Almeida joins Matt Stephens for a brilliantly honest conversation about the life and mindset of one of the most consistent GC riders in the modern peloton. Matt also explores what it is like sharing a team with superstars Tadej Pogačar, Juan Ayuso, and rising talent Isaac del Toro. Does riding for UAE Team Emirates limit João’s personal ambitions, or unlock even more potential? What did he really say in that infamous Tom Pidcock moment? João answers all of this and more with refreshing honesty. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices.

item.title: 145. Alistair Brownlee: Triathlon Legend, Gravel Racing Demon, and Sportsmanship Superstar
item.description: This week James and Will are joined by Alistair Brownlee, the legendary triathlete turned Ironman champion turned gravel racing extraordinaire turned sports scientist turned... If there were a personification of the polymath endurance athlete, Alistair would be it: from being the only triathlete to hold two Olympic individual triathlon titles, to sacrificing his own race to carry his exhausted brother Jonny across the finish line, to starting his own sports nutrition company, TrueFuels, to finishing in the top ten at some of the world’s most notorious gravel events, and more. At this point, we should probably just say listen to the episode. Interview begins at 9:00. This episode is brought to you by the Hammerhead Karoo GPS bike computer. Visit hammerhead.io and use the code CYCLIST to get a free HR strap with every purchase, just be sure to add the strap to your cart before applying the code at checkout. This episode is also brought to you by Help for Heroes Big Battlefield Bike Ride, an epic four-day, 250-mile trip around Belgium and Northern France that raises money for Armed Forces veterans and their families. See helpforheroes.org.uk for more. Did you know Cyclist is also a stunning monthly print magazine? Subscribe now at store.cyclist.co.uk/cycpod and get every issue for less than in the shops, delivered straight to your door. It is also a lovely website about everything road cycling and gravel. Check us out at cyclist.co.uk. Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: 394: Gregaard on Career Retirement, and Is a Cyclist Denmark’s Sports Name of the Year?
item.description: The season is drawing to a close, and for Jonas Gregaard, his professional career is also over at just 29 years old. We talk to Høvlen from Herlev about the highs and lows, and the reason for the early retirement. Three Danes — Jonas Vingegaard, Mads P, and Niklas Larsen — are nominated for Sports Name of the Year, and we quiz each other on the sports names of history, with somewhat surprising results. Veloropa Podcast is presented by Aioss (aioss.dk, use the code velotest), HelloFresh (hellofresh.dk), and Power (avis.power.dk).

item.title: Gregaard on Career Retirement, and Is a Cyclist Denmark’s Sports Name of the Year?
S1 Ep672: Cyclists Should Be Allowed to Run Red Lights
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we dive into a controversial debate: is it time to legalize cyclists running red lights and stop signs? We also look at a bonkers new "6-in-1" GPS head unit that uses AI and inflates your tires, and we analyze the highest VO2 max potentially ever recorded. Finally, we discuss why more than 6,000 people have signed a petition to change the route of the Tour de France.

item.title: Ambitions
item.description: Listen to the full episode on Podimo.dk/eddy. National coach Mørkøv visits Café Eddy, and the elliptical treadmill of the late-modern world that is the six-day race is examined closely. The conversation sparks when the question is asked: What is a domestique? And then it is time to find out what Maitre Jacques would think of Amager. Hosts: Brian Holm and Bastian Emil. Guests: Michael Mørkøv and Edie Choleva. Producer: Andreas 'Lydmand' Saxild. Editor: Sarah Ørsted. Listen to Café Eddy exclusively on Podimo. Get a good offer at podimo.dk/eddy. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: I Love Westegnen Pesto
item.description: With a view of Corsica on one side and the back of Rib House on the other, Mads and Jakob meet for the 35th episode of Lang Distance. They hand out this week’s style award to 400 km of forest riding in November, the gravel team time trial that went wrong, a comeback for Gribben, the C3 rearing up with all the snacks in the trunk, a shout-out to Magnus and Thomas’ new gravel podcast, and finally a bit about trust in your coach, therapist, and bike fitter. That and much more in this episode of Lang Distance.

item.title: The best gravel bikes at human prices
item.description: Many recreational riders want to try gravel cycling, or upgrade the gravel bike they already have. In this episode of Bagerstop, you can hear the best advice for buying a gravel bike at a reasonably affordable price. Where do you get the best value for money, and what should you prioritize in terms of components and extras? Victor Malgaard Ottesen and Jonas Lindberg give their top three gravel bike picks for up to 12,000 DKK and three good options for gravel bikes at 24,000 DKK. Support the show.

item.title: S1 Ep671: I'm 42, Should I Know Better?
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, Si explains the story behind his cuts and bruises after a nasty cyclocross crash. We investigate a new report claiming cycling is the most dangerous sport in America, ahead of basketball and football. Plus, Mathieu van der Poel may be taking on an Ironman, and we check out the world's first aluminum velodrome.

item.title: The Man in the Hole
item.description: Listen to the full episode on Podimo.dk/eddy. Rasmus Bruun joins the show, and the conversation turns to Greek mythology, hero worship, strengths, weaknesses, TV series, the Big Bang, walking and eating at the same time, bullfighting, a bit about brave cyclists, poetry, and much more. Hosts: Brian Holm and Bastian Emil. Guests: Edie Choleva and Rasmus Bruun. Producer: Andreas 'Lydmand' Saxild. Editor: Sarah Ørsted. Listen to Café Eddy exclusively on Podimo. Get a good offer at podimo.dk/eddy. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: About the toilet
item.description: On a connection from freezing-cold Denmark to the mild autumn weather of Monaco, we’re back with another episode of Lang Distance. A bit about 'bolo gate,' a fresh midwife, brake lights on a run, golden nuggets from Micki Lynge, support Danish cycling, Pløgelse from now on, train a bit more - it’s not that hard, can Pappa P drive the wrong way, Ignis gets this week’s blunder, who survives longest in Alone in the Wilderness, and much more in this episode of Lang Distance.

item.title: Chris Hall on his silly bike challenges for Movember
item.description: Matt Stephens chats with ultra-distance bike racer, content creator, and charity fundraiser Chris Hall about his recent Movember challenges. Despite atrocious weather conditions, Chris completed an unsupported lap of Britain during the summer of 2025, learning valuable lessons along the way. With a history of severe depression, Movember is a charity close to his heart, and this monumental effort is documented superbly in Chris’ film, 'Bikepacking the Perimeter of Britain.' Cycling has played a huge part in Chris' recovery from depression and continues to help him maintain his mental health. www.sigmasports.com Support Chris’ Movember fundraising at his MoSpace: https://uk.movember.com/mospace/6408721 Chris Hall’s 'Bikepacking The Perimeter of Britain' on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FLGuamHY2pc Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices

item.title: #22 Bak Lane: Frank Schleck
item.description: Lars Bak visits his old friend and teammate Frank Schleck for a chat about the old days at Team CSC and their time together in Luxembourg. What has Frank been doing since retiring? And what is the new project he will be part of in 2026 on Lidl-Trek's women's team, where he will become sports director for Danish rider Emma Norsgaard Bjerg? Sit back and join Bak Lane. Host: Lars Bak. Produced by Anders Mielke & Forhjulslir.

item.title: 144. Emma Pooley: Tour de France winner, Oat to Joy author and pro-athlete sage
item.description: This week, Robyn and Will are joined by Emma Pooley, the 2009 Tour de France Femmes winner (then called the Grande Boucle Féminine), Olympic medallist, UCI world time trial champion, multiple national road and time trial champion, and triathlon and duathlon champion, as well as a first-class Cambridge graduate and University Challenge contestant. Oh, and Emma also won the Brompton bike racing world championships and held the Everesting record in 2020 - 8,848 meters climbed in 8 hours 53 minutes 36 seconds, or ten back-to-back loops of a 6.8 km climb at 13% plus descents. Incredible. In this episode, Emma talks about the psychological challenges of being a pro, the pressure to conform to ideals such as riders' weight, her highs and lows throughout her career, and her new book, Oat to Joy, in which she explains the role of oats in a cyclist's diet, along with recipes and stories from her life on a bike. The interview begins at 5:16. Emma Pooley's Oat to Joy, a recipe book with stories from her cycling life, is available now at emmapooley.net. This episode is brought to you by the Hammerhead Karoo GPS bike computer. Visit hammerhead.io and use the code CYCLIST to get a free HR strap with every purchase (just be sure to add the strap to your cart, then apply the code at checkout). Did you know Cyclist is also a stunning monthly print magazine? Subscribe now at store.cyclist.co.uk/cycpod and get every issue for less than in the shops, delivered straight to your door. And it's also a lovely website about everything road cycling and gravel. Check us out at cyclist.co.uk. Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: Radio Tour - In the breakaway: Season awards and Søjberg's French adventure
item.description: The off-season has arrived, and Anders Mielke reflects on this year's cycling season. He does so in the company of Anders Lund and Rasmus Søjberg Pedersen.

item.title: S1 Ep670: 5 Reasons Why Gravel Suspension Is Inevitable
item.description: Is full suspension the inevitable future of gravel cycling? With Trek launching a new full-suspension gravel bike, the GCN Show presenters debate five reasons why we'll all end up on one, and the arguments against it. Plus, we cover the latest cycling news, including a major bike recall, Mathieu van der Poel's 'Best Cyclist' crown, and the end of Gent-Wevelgem as we know it.

item.title: Another Time
item.description: Listen to the full episode on Podimo.dk/eddy. Café Eddy is joined by Edie and the show’s chef SK, and the conversation turns to lifetime bans, entering the big mountains, weddings and growing families before Maitre Jacques weighs in on the North Sea and prepping, and Brian расскаls how a bigger, smaller drama at home almost cost him his good mood. Hosts: Brian Holm and Bastian Emil. Guests: Edie Choleva and Speed King. Producers: Andreas 'Lydmand' Saxild and Jakob Ranum. Editor: Sarah Ørsted. Listen to Café Eddy exclusively on Podimo. Get a good offer at podimo.dk/eddy. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Somewhere Between a Handyman and an American
item.description: We’re back with video again, once more brought to you in collaboration with Nitor Energy. Jakob is also very happy with his handyman bottle and handyman bottle holder. Who actually takes the train from Eskilstrup to Sløgelse, the C3 is idling, a special maternity-leave episode is on the way, Mads has a broken hose, and it’s still no thanks to red cabbage in the flute. That and much more in this episode of Lang Distance.

item.title: The Big Test of Electronic Mini Pumps
item.description: Electronic mini pumps are becoming increasingly popular with Danish cyclists. In this episode of Bagerstop, we test seven of the smart pumps, from MugOff to Silca and Cycplus. The test shows a big difference in pumping capacity and pumping speed. Support the show

item.title: The Big Test of Electronic Mini Pumps
item.description: Electronic mini pumps are becoming increasingly popular with Danish cyclists. In this episode of Bagerstop, we test seven of the smart pumps, from MugOff to Silca and Cycplus. The test shows a big difference in pumping capacity and pumping speed. Support the show

item.title: He’s Clearly Smarter Than He Looks
item.description: Up in Norway there lived… no, wait, Mads was just on holiday and now he’s back. A fresh episode and a fresh stone chip in the RS 6. We follow up on last week’s visit to Nitor Energy, which this week’s episode is brought to you in collaboration with. We cover a possible blunder of the decade, diapers, a bit more about diapers, the latest news from the autobahn, invention of the year, and much more in this episode of Lang Distance. We apologize for the missing video on the podcast — it will be back next week.

item.title: S1 Ep669: Are Exhibition Races Good for Cycling?
item.description: This week on the GCN Show, we dive into the weird world of predetermined races—should fixed post-Tour criteriums be banished to the history books, or are they a bit of fun that should be celebrated? We discuss the origins of these races, share our own experiences, and debate the end-of-season ASO-run criteriums in Singapore and Japan. Plus, we've got a new law in Canada that could make it illegal to drop your child off to school on a bike, a new massive gravel stage race, and Conor tries to sell Dan on the UK's first "boozy bike trail".

item.title: About Acting and Film
item.description: Listen to the full episode on Podimo.dk/eddy Actor Paw Henriksen visits Café Eddy, and the discussion covers Oier Lazkano’s doping case, shared captain roles, and riders’ off-season duties. Then they talk about films, actors, directors, popcorn, cinemas, forgotten lines, and Mike Tyson quotes. Hosts: Brian Holm and Bastian Emil. Guests: Edie Choleva and Paw Henriksen. Producer: Andreas 'Lydmand' Saxild. Editor: Sarah Ørsted. Listen to Café Eddy exclusively on Podimo. Get a great offer at podimo.dk/eddy Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: 393: Track Worlds, Tour Route, Lazkano and Uno-X
item.description: It’s been a while since the last episode, so there’s a lot to cover. We look at the recently concluded Track World Championships in Chile, where Rasmus Lund, among other things, talks about Denmark’s men’s 4,000-meter team winning their third straight world title. We try to explain what the case against Oier Lazkano is about, take an early look at this year’s Tour route, which again includes 3x Montmartre and this time also 2x Alpe d’Huez. Norwegian Uno-X gained access to the promised World Tour land, and Plesner presents a spectacular, but also surprising, Power Ranking with suspense, Slovenian blinkers, and a bit of Danish-hat energy... Veloropa Podcast is presented by the Danish supplement Aioss (use code velotest at aioss.dk), meal kits from HelloFresh, and electronics chain Power (power.dk/x).

item.title: Track Worlds, Tour Route, Lazkano and Uno-X
#21 Bak Lane: Rune Kristensen
item.description: In the world of cycling, some people get up earlier than everyone else and go to bed when the rest of the team is asleep. They are the mechanics. The invisible heroes behind every gear change, every wheel swap, and every victory. In this episode, Lars Bak travels to Luxembourg to visit his good friend Rune Kristensen. With 18 years of experience as a mechanic on some of the world’s biggest teams — Team CSC, Saxo Bank, Leopard Trek, Quick-Step, and INEOS — Rune has seen the sport from the back seat, the van, and hotel parking lots across Europe. We dive into life on the road: hectic days, technical miracles, stars up close, and the stories that rarely make it to camera. Host: Lars Bak. Produced by Anders Mielke (Forhjulslir).

item.title: Emma Pooley on Competitiveness, Privilege and Roast Potatoes
item.description: Matt Stephens chats to Emma Pooley about her incredibly varied career, which spans professional cycling, long-distance running and triathlon, as well as a University Challenge victory and some Geotech Engineering for good measure. A true multi-disciplinary champion, Emma speaks candidly about her struggle to retire from professional sport and her enduring love of the mountains. The former UCI individual time trial world champion and Olympic silver medalist shares the origin story of her competitive nature, which, to Matt’s surprise, involves roast potatoes. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices

item.title: 143. Why Are the Pros So Fast, and How Can We Go Faster? with Dr Xavier Disley
item.description: This week, James is joined by Dr Xavier Disley: sports scientist, aerodynamicist, national masters track champion, and founder of AeroCoach, a UK collective of coaches, scientists, and engineers all focused on one thing: making cyclists faster. Xavier has worked with WorldTour teams, winners of all three Grand Tours, track champions, Hour Record breakers, component manufacturers, and clothing brands, all with the same goal of going faster. In this conversation, he explains the basics of aerodynamics and the pursuit of speed from the ground up: the underlying principles, how rider and bike work together, common mistakes that slow people down, the best value upgrades, and the holy grail of how to go faster for free. It’s a technical topic, but Xavier makes it brilliantly relatable, so put the kettle on and settle into your favourite chair — or onto your turbo, whatever works for you. Interview begins at 7:03. This episode is brought to you by the Hammerhead Karoo GPS bike computer. Visit hammerhead.io and use code CYCLIST to get a free HR strap with every purchase; just add the strap to your cart before applying the code at checkout. Cyclist is also a monthly print magazine. Subscribe at store.cyclist.co.uk/cycpod to get every issue for less than in the shops, delivered to your door. You can also find Cyclist online at cyclist.co.uk. Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: Short Distance: I Just Need 10
item.description: For the first time in the podcast’s history, Mads and Jakob are together in person — a kind of “Short Distance.” Join this “reportage special” as we head to Aarhus to visit this week’s sponsor, Nitor Energy. Come along for a ride in the RS6, get an update on Molslinjen, a quick check-in with Bundgaard, some atmosphere from the bike and from Nitor Energy, and we wrap up with reflections from the journey home on Route 21 — “what’s it doing?” Featuring Jakob Egholm, Mads Pedersen, and Kristian Egholm (sound engineer).

item.title: Cycling Journalism
item.description: Listen to the full episode on Podimo.dk/eddy. Café Eddy is joined by Rasmus Nowak from feltet.dk, and the World Championships, Fernando Pessoa, Lance Armstrong, the friendly Norwegians, and much more are discussed before the classic debates about Maitre Jacques and God Verdomme really get going. Hosts: Bastian Emil and Brian Holm. Guests: Rasmus Nowak and Edie Choleva. Producer: Andreas 'Lydmand' Saxild. Editor: Andreas Lindinger Saxild. Listen to Café Eddy exclusively on Podimo. Get a good offer at podimo.dk/eddy. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: Radio Tour - Breakaway: Contract Expiry and Career Choices with Andreas Stokbro and Rasmus Bøgh Wallin
item.description: The regular cycling season is now over, giving Andreas Stokbro and Rasmus Bøgh Wallin time to evaluate their performances and think about the future. Watch Radio Tour - i udbrud on HBO Max.

item.title: S1 Ep667: Change Is Coming — Are You Ready for It?
item.description: The cycling world is changing fast. This week we dive into the big debates: should electric cargo bikes share your local cycle lanes, and are full-suspension gravel bikes the future? We also catch up on the latest pro cycling drama, including the surprise end to the Strava vs. Garmin lawsuit, the latest team mergers and closures, and some truly incredible record-breaking rides. WIN 👉 https://gcn.eu/GCNHoverAirCompetition

item.title: Grand Prix L'automne
item.description: Listen to the full episode on Podimo.dk/eddy. National coach Mørkøv joins Café Eddy, and the autumn and the season’s final races are discussed before the focus turns to winter and the track. Hosts: Bastian Emil and Brian Holm. Guest: Michael Mørkøv and Edie Choleva. Producer: Jakob Ranum. Editor: Sarah Ørsted. Listen to Café Eddy exclusively on Podimo. Get a good offer at podimo.dk/eddy. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: A Child’s Seat on Four Wheels
item.description: The autumn holiday mood has settled in for Jakob and Mads. We start with the latest musical contribution from the podcast’s house musician Henrik Solgård, aka Lars. To celebrate 10,000 followers on Instagram, we’re giving away one Lang Distance “Det suger” T-shirt — leave a comment saying which episode you like best. The winner will be contacted directly. We get this week’s recommendations, this week’s gear, and this week’s blunder. Mads also brings this week’s joke, which may possibly be the end of Lang Distance. At the end of the episode, we tease the “Short Distance” episode we’ll record next week. Listen in!

item.title: Bagerstop Listeners Have Spoken: Here Are the Best WorldTour Bikes
item.description: 250 Bagerstop listeners have rated the bikes of the 18 WorldTour teams, and here you get the result of the big poll. The two bike nerds, Frederik Muff and Kasper Sømod Jensen, join as judges and experts, and they also rate the many bikes themselves. Listen in and find out whether the result surprises you. Support the show.

item.title: #20 Bak Lane: Marcel Sieberg
item.description: Bak Lane has headed south to Bocholt, Germany, where former professional cyclist and current sports director at Tudor Pro Cycling, Marcel Sieberg, joins the podcast. Lars Bak and Sieberg spent eight seasons together on the HTC and Lotto teams, where they were part of one of the world’s most feared sprint trains. With his long frame and calm composure, Sieberg was the ideal pilot and lead-out rider for the legendary German sprinter André Greipel. Join Bak and Sieberg on a ride down memory lane as they share stories from their years in the peloton and look back on the career of one of the hardest-working and most underrated support riders of his era, who spent much of his time in Greipel’s shadow but played a crucial role in many of his victories. Host: Lars Bak. Note: This episode is in English. Bak Lane is part of Forhjulslir. Edited by Anders Mielke.

item.title: 142. Magnus Cort: the Uno-X Pro on Grand Tours, Classics, and Dyeing His Moustache Blue
item.description: This week, Robyn — and Will for the intro — is joined by Uno-X’s expert hotel reviewer and star racer, Magnus Cort. A stage winner at all three Grand Tours, Magnus is enjoying a new lease of life in the Classics, with recent podiums at both Eschborn-Frankfurt and the Bretagne Classic, plus 6th place at Milan-San Remo this season. In this episode, the Dane talks about his move from stage hunter to one-day specialist, how he became a social media star, dyeing his moustache blue for a bet, the Monument he dreams of winning, and his love of breakaways. Interview begins at 3:20. This episode is brought to you by the Hammerhead Karoo GPS bike computer. Visit hammerhead.io and use code CYCLIST to get a free HR strap with every purchase; just add the strap to your cart before applying the code at checkout. Cyclist is also a monthly print magazine. Subscribe at store.cyclist.co.uk/cycpod to get every issue for less than in the shops, delivered to your door. You can also find Cyclist online at cyclist.co.uk. Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

item.title: S1 Ep666: Pogačar Finally Beaten... by GCN
item.description: The race we’ve all been waiting for finally happened: Tadej Pogačar took on GCN’s own “best climber” and lawyer, Andrew Feather, at the Pogi Challenge. We break down exactly how it went, along with the latest news from the Gravel World Championships, a look at fascinating new cycling tech and regulations, and a recap of a major milestone in Pogačar’s career. Tune in for the news, hacks and bodges, and an update on the Zwift Racing League teams. Win the HOVERAir X1 PROMAX Cycling Bundle: https://gcn.eu/GCNHoverAirCompetition

item.title: Autumn Fever and Rolf Sørensen
item.description: Listen to the rest of the episode at podimo.dk/eddy. Rolf visits Café Eddy, where the conversation turns to the end of the season, the arrival of autumn, Il Lombardia, Paris-Tours, well-laid tables, and raised little fingers. Host: Edie Choleva. Guests: Bastian Emil, Brian Holm, and Rolf Sørensen. Producer: Andreas “Lydmand” Saxild. Editor: Sarah Ørsted. Listen to Café Eddy exclusively on Podimo. Get a good offer at podimo.dk/eddy. Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.title: 392: Pogacar Finishes on Top, Albert Impresses, and Uno-X Nears the WT
item.description: Tadej Pogacar, as expected, took his fifth straight victory and made history by becoming the first rider ever to finish in the top three in all of the season’s Monuments. UAE have also set a new benchmark for wins in a season, with 94 so far, but with a bit of luck and greed they may even reach 100. Uno-X are hoping for wins before Sunday as they battle Cofidis for what is likely the final WorldTour spot next year. And next year looks very promising for Danish talent Albert Philipsen, who has capped off a superb end to the season, most recently with a podium in the classic Paris-Tours. Veloropa Podcast is presented in collaboration with the investment platform Pluto Markets (https://pluto.markets) and electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk).

item.title: Mads Speeding Ticket
item.description: We’re back in collaboration with Philips OneBlade Face & Body. Listen in and find out how well the OneBlade handled the hair on Jakob’s legs, or where the 360-degree feature took Mads. We go through this week’s gear, which has three takers this time. We hear whether there’s any news from Mads’ fake carpenter brothers, updates on merch, “always ride with two clips,” and “it sucks” has caught on in other podcasts. We wrap up with a Klamphugger edit of the podcast’s own Henrik Solgård, namely Djan Carnary (also known as Lars). Note: the competition for one Trek home is first come, first served, and the winner has been found.

item.title: Amy Hudson on World Mental Health Day 2025
item.description: Matt Stephens speaks with Amy Hudson, known on social media as amy.cycling.adventures, about her struggles with mental health and the role cycling has played in her recovery. While working as a mental health nurse for the NHS, Amy began experiencing anxiety and eating disorders, along with suicidal thoughts. Her husband bought her a road bike, and it slowly changed her life. Now, with the goal of raising funds for mental health charities, she has completed the entire men’s Tour de France route as well as the transfers, and is setting her sights on a huge challenge in 2026. www.sigmasports.com. Amy’s fundraising page for Shout and The Mix mental health charities: https://www.justgiving.com/page/a-c-a. item.title: 391: Record-Breaking Pogacar Ready for Lombardia
item.description: Mads P withdrew, Jonas Vingegaard was dropped with more than 100 kilometers to go in Sunday’s European Championship race in France, and Mattias Skjelmose had to fight almost on his own to a respectable seventh place in a race where Tadej Pogacar set new standards for a solo win in a championship race and where Remco Evenepoel once again had to admit that he still has a long way to go before he can truly challenge the double world champion, at least in road races. We look back at the European Championships and discuss, among other things, Jonas Vingegaard’s performance and Belgium’s tactics, then look ahead to Saturday’s Il Lombardia, the final Monument of the year, where Pogacar can break another record if he wins for the fifth time in a row. Veloropa Podcast is presented by the investment platform Pluto Markets (pluto.markets) and electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk).

item.title: S1 Ep665: Is This the End of Strava? Strava Sues Garmin
item.description: Strava is suing Garmin and demanding that it stop selling nearly all of its fitness and cycling products, a shocking move against its biggest long-term partner. We dig into this major lawsuit, breaking down what Strava is claiming, what Garmin is asking for, and the real-world impact this corporate conflict could have on your riding and your favorite apps. Plus, we cover major ultra-endurance news, an unbelievable change to UK bike theft policy, new tech from Specialized, and all the latest racing action.

item.title: S1 Ep664: The UCI Have Unbanned Stuff! What’s Next?
item.description: The UCI has made some U-turns. This week, we look at the rule reversal on handlebar width and the ban on time-trial helmets in road races: is it a positive step, or are they still not helping themselves? We also celebrate Tadej Pogačar’s historic season and discuss Trek’s surprising new full-suspension gravel bike. Finally, don’t miss our reaction to the upset at the Women’s World Road Race Championships and the latest updates from the Zwift Racing League. Check out Project Cycle Africa: https://gcn.eu/ProjectCycleAfrica

item.title: Can You Get by with Just One Bike for Gravel and Road?
item.description: Can you manage with just one bike for both gravel and road? And what happens when you test an aero bike against an aero gravel bike and an endurance gravel bike, all with exactly the same tires, wheels, and groupset? That’s what we test in this episode. Jonas Lindberg explains the differences between the various bike models, and we test three different models from the same brand to put numbers behind the assumptions. Support the show.

item.title: #19 Bak Lane: Carsten Jeppesen
item.description: Baklane is back with a technical, nerdy deep dive into what often decides the difference between victory and defeat in modern cycling: equipment, data, and marginal gains. Lars Bak is joined by Carsten Jeppesen, Director of Performance Operations at Team INEOS Grenadiers, where he has worked for 16 years. Carsten has been a constant part of cycling’s development over several decades, first at CSC, Saxo Bank, and later at Team Sky/INEOS, where the British powerhouse set a new standard for performance. Bak and Carsten know each other from their time at Team CSC and Saxo Bank, and together they look back at how the sport has changed, where the line lies between technology and talent, and what the future holds for WorldTour teams. Host: Lars Bak. Baklane is part of Forhjulslir. Edited by: Anders Mielke / Forhjulslir.

item.title: 141. Joe Friel: Train slow to race fast, and why Pogačar is such a beast
item.description: This week, James is joined by cycling coaching guru Joe Friel. Joe began coaching amateur and elite riders in the US in the 1980s, before becoming a household name in 1996 with The Cyclist's Training Bible, arguably the most successful cycling training guide ever and still in print today in its fifth edition. In 1999, Joe founded TrainingPeaks with his son Dirk and friend Gear Fisher, the training and analysis platform that many would agree still sets the standard for digital coaching tools, used by WorldTour pros and amateurs alike. This summer, Joe released his new book, High-Performance Cyclist, and here he discusses many of its themes, including how riding slower can make you faster, measuring progress with one simple metric, and the importance of sleep and eating strategies for weight loss. Joe Friel's latest book, High Performance Cyclist (Bloomsbury), is available to buy here. Interview begins at 6:07. This episode is brought to you by the Hammerhead Karoo GPS bike computer. item.title: To unshaven baboons
item.description: This week’s episode is brought to you in partnership with Philips, and a fresh shave was much needed. We cover all the places the Philips OneBlade Face & Body can be used, snip snap, and Mads’s arms are mirror-smooth for the European Championship time trial. The C3 runs on insults, Mads would rather take a swing to the head than ride French, there’s a bit about the best meat whistle in Sløgelse, and much more in this episode of Lang Distance.

item.title: 390: The European Championships, the World Championships, and Jørgen
item.description: In this episode, we remember Jørgen Leth, look back on yet another dominant World Championship victory for another legend of cycling, Tadej Pogacar, and look ahead to this week’s European Championships, where in-form Mattias Skjelmose will be joined by Jonas Vingegaard and Mads P as France hosts the road races. Veloropa Podcast is presented in collaboration with meal kits from HelloFresh (hellofresh.dk) and the electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk).

item.title: Freedom flies
item.description: Edie Choleva and Matti Breschel join the café, and the recently concluded World Championships in Rwanda are discussed. The conversation turns to art that leaves a lasting impression, drone and Strava activity, island communities and freedom, while Brian reveals how you should not drink from a large bottle. Hosts: Brian Holm and Bastian Emil. Guests: Matti Breschel and Edie Choleva. Producer: Andreas 'Lydmand' Saxild. Editor: Trine Schou. Listen to Café Eddy exclusively on Podimo. item.title: Have you had a long stop on driving and rest time in Roskilde?
item.description: While the World Championships in Rwanda are in full swing, Mads and Jakob sit down for a podcast, where Mads gives his pick for a top 10 in the men’s World Championship road race. We also finally settle once and for all whether Mads has any connection to the carpenter twins, Papa P joins on the line, there are new Lang Distance songs, hunting or yacht talk, updates on merch, and much more in this episode of Lang Distance.

item.title: Filippo Conca on how winning the Maglia Tricolore changed his life
item.description: Filippo Conca races in the Maglia Tricolore with UCI WorldTeam Jayco–AlUla, but just three months ago that seemed like a distant dream. Out of contract at the end of 2024, he found a lifeline with SWATT Club, an Italian amateur team known for giving cyclists a fresh start through a mix of road and gravel racing. In this podcast, Filippo explains what it feels like to win the Italian national road race championship as what he calls a 'normal rider', how his engine suited gravel racing, what he has planned for the end of this season, and his goals for 2026. His story is one of resilience, redemption, and victory — almost like a movie. www.sigmasports.com EXCLUSIVE NordVPN Deal ➼ https://nordvpn.com/unplugged Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices

item.title: 389: The big World Championship preview
item.description: The World Championships in Rwanda are underway, and the first road World Championships in Africa got off to a sensational start when Remco Evenepoel caught and dropped Tadej Pogacar by more than two and a half minutes in the time trial. On Sunday, the two meet again in a rematch in the road race, where Denmark is also among the outsiders, led by captain Skjelmose. We look at the major teams, the duel between Belgium and Slovenia, the big favourites, and the brutal World Championship course with almost 5,500 metres of climbing. And as if that were not enough, we also have Lasse Schøber from Mandsholdet on this episode. Lasse is one of the few who has been down to inspect the World Championship route in Rwanda and has helped national coach Michael Mørkøv and the Danish riders with important recon work. Veloropa Podcast is presented in collaboration with the Danish supplement Aioss (aioss.dk - use the code veloropa) and the electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk).

item.title: 10 kilometers for time: Hacks for extra speed and Denmark’s fastest influencer
item.description: The rising cycling influencer Gustav Toft Simonsen takes on Frederik Muff’s special challenge: ride 10 kilometers as fast as possible. Muff gives Gustav advice — and also the rest of us who dream of getting a little faster on the road. We start out on the road in an ID4 in Gustav Toft Simonsen’s wake, and finish with a conversation about speed on the bike and 23-year-old Gustav’s journey from business owner to a fully committed B-license racer. Support the show.

item.title: S1 Ep663: What separates the good from the great?
item.description: Is being a champion just about power numbers and genetics, or is there more to it? We explore what truly separates the best in the peloton from the rest, drawing on our experience as professional cyclists. We also discuss the latest cycling news, from Strava’s insane valuation to the UCI’s legal challenges, and share some of the best cycling hacks and bodges submitted by you! item.description: After a summer with the Tour de France and Vuelta a España, Baklane is finally back. item.title: I've already arranged a date with Vingegaard
item.description: We get off to a hard start after the Vuelta with "Long Distance Song," hand out the week's style points and blunders, Mads' financial corner ft. Jacob Ditzel, what awaits when you get home from a Grand Tour, people watching on YouTube, a podium ceremony in the spirit of Long Distance on the coolers, and much more in this episode of Lang Distance.

item.title: 140. Kasia Niewiadoma Phinney, Tour de France champion and Canyon-SRAM zondacrypto star
item.description: This week, Robyn, and James for the intro, is joined by Kasia Niewiadoma Phinney, winner of the 2024 Tour de France Femmes and an all-around star rider for Canyon–SRAM zondacrypto. Kasia can win stage races, one-day races, Classics, national titles, world titles—you name it, the Polish rider has a trophy and jersey for it. Here, Kasia speaks candidly about dealing with disappointment at this year's TDFF, where she finished third, her changing definition of success, her fearlessness as a rider, her hopes for the upcoming World Championships in Rwanda, and the legacy she hopes she and her fellow riders will leave for future generations of cyclists. Interview begins at 7:43. This episode is brought to you by the Hammerhead Karoo GPS bike computer. item.title: 388: Vingegaard finishes in style on a brutal climb
item.description: Jonas Vingegaard put a fine finishing touch on the brutal Bola del Mundo climb, which decided this year's Vuelta a España and secured Denmark's first-ever Vuelta victory. Despite illness along the way, Vingegaard fought his way to his third Grand Tour win on Spanish roads, while Mads P completed the Danish triumph by riding to a dominant victory in the points classification. Tadej Pogacar, Vingegaard's Tour de France rival, showed in Canada that he is fully ready for the World Championships in Rwanda, and Mattias Skjelmose did too, after his crash in Maryland. item.title: 387: Nothing Decided Before the Final Weekend of the Vuelta
item.description: Elming has been on a trip with Bygma in Spain, where Mads P is very close to securing the green points jersey in the Vuelta a España after finishing second on Friday’s stage, won by Jasper Philipsen after excellent lead-out work from his teammates. But in the general classification, Jonas Vingegaard is only 44 seconds ahead of João Almeida before Saturday’s tough mountain stage, where anything can happen. Still, if the stage follows the rest of the Vuelta’s script, there may not be major gaps between the race’s two big favorites. We also talk about a Mexican bull showing great form in Italy, and we preview the weekend’s World Tour race in Canada, the Grand Prix Montréal, where Tadej Pogačar will line up as the defending champion. The Veloropa Podcast is presented in collaboration with Bygma (bygma.dk), meal kits from HelloFresh (hellofresh.dk), and the electronics chain Power (avis.power.dk).

item.title: I Just Knocked Back a Liter of Cheap Homebrew Fuel
item.description: We kick off hard with the Spanish commentary on Mads’ win. After a couple of days with a cold, Mads and Lang Distance are back on top again. We cover a lot of ground in this episode and, of course, talk about the Vuelta, a Chris Anker highlight, the bet about Ciccone’s Ferrari, “they’re wild,” “Merch Preben,” and the funniest content our listeners send us—please keep it coming.

item.title: S1 Ep661: Proof That Riding More and Riding Slower Makes You Faster
item.description: Geraint Thomas and Ellen van Dijk are stepping away from professional cycling, but their long careers are proof of the benefits of consistent training. This week, we look at new evidence suggesting that long, easy rides are the key to success, check out the latest tech from Wahoo, round up the latest cycling news, and cover a new world record attempt. item.title: Mountain Biking at 40, 50, and Beyond with Lindsey Richter
item.description: I first interviewed Lindsey Richter five years ago, and that episode became one of the most popular I’ve ever done. Like anyone who has been to one of Lindsey’s Ladies All Ride skills camps, listeners immediately fell in love with her. So I was obviously thrilled to have Lindsey back on the podcast for a second time. Lindsey is now 50 and stronger and wiser than ever. She just returned from leading an all-women train down A-Line at Crankworx Whistler, and she continues to help other women develop the same confident, badass energy she embodies. In this interview, we talk about mountain biking as we get older, but this episode is also for anyone who has ever ridden a mountain bike or wants to get on one. Lindsey shares advice for women who are brand new to the sport, how to overcome mental hurdles, why building community matters so much, and much more. Full show notes: https://femmecyclist.com/lindsey-richter-interview 💜 Connect with Lindsey Website: https://ladiesallride.com/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/ladiesallride/ ❤️ Join Kristen & the Femme Cyclist community ➡︎ Website: https://femmecyclist.com ➡︎ YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/femmecyclist ➡︎ Instagram: https://instagram.com/femme_cyclist ➡︎ Facebook: https://facebook.com/femmecyclist Support the show 👉 Instagram: @femme_cyclist 👉 Website: https://femmecyclist.com

item.title: The Future of E-Bikes with Denmark’s Secret Cycling Engineer
item.description: Morten Kristiansen has designed bikes at Cannondale, one line at a time. He has been head of Ridley, helped Bosch with its E-Bike System, and is now CEO of a Spanish e-bike manufacturer. In this episode of Bagerstop, you can meet the “secret” Danish cycling engineer with an impressive track record in the global cycling industry. Morten also shares his thoughts on the future of e-bikes and explains why the UCI has recently intensified its hunt for motor doping. The audio could be better in this episode. Apparently, Granada is a long way away, at least according to the tech. Support the show

item.title: 139. Tom Simpson: World Champion and Cycling Legend, with Chris Sidwells
item.description: This week, James is joined by cycling journalist and author Chris Sidwells to explore the life and times of Britain’s most storied rider, Tom Simpson, on the eve of the 60th anniversary of Simpson’s historic World Championships win. As Simpson’s nephew, Chris is uniquely placed to tell the story of the miner’s son from County Durham who became the first Briton to wear the yellow jersey and won countless races, including three of the five Monuments, along the way. As most will know, the end of Simpson’s story is a tragic one, but while Chris speaks candidly about that day on Mont Ventoux and the events leading up to it, he is also here to remember Simpson as the trailblazing, mercurial rider whose palmarès deserves to be what we remember him for most. So sit back and enjoy stories you may have heard before, told with unique insight, plus many more you haven’t, as we celebrate the extraordinary feats of Mr. Tom. From 7th to 14th September, Chris and his Cycling Legends friends will host an extended Tom Simpson Cycling Festival in Harworth, Nottinghamshire, including sportives, cycling talks and films, the reopening of the Tom Simpson Museum, and the return of the Retro Weekender, featuring the Simpson Retro Ride, Best in Show vintage bike competition, and all kinds of cycle-focused fun, from grass-track racing to the retro bike jumble. See cyclinglegends.co.uk. Interview begins at 5:40. ----------------- This episode is brought to you by the Hammerhead Karoo GPS bike computer. item.title: S1 Ep658: Does Cycling Have a Problem With Elitism and Snobbery?
item.description: Welcome to the GCN Show! This week, we issue another apology after viewers criticized our perceived snobbery in the last episode. We ask whether cycling has a broader problem with being unfriendly and elitist, and if so, what can be done about it. item.title: Tobias Hansen: From Cardiac Arrest to Mountain Summit
item.description: Tobias Hansen died on 16 May 2025. In the dunes of northwest Jutland, he collapsed with his sons Mio and Hugo by his side. Fortunately, Hugo quickly ran for help, and Tobias Hansen was revived. When Tobias woke from his coma, he was back on the bike. At first only on the home trainer, but by late June he was riding the roads of Bornholm - and on 23 July he climbed Mont Ventoux. Two months after his cardiac arrest, he stood on top of one of Europe’s toughest mountains to conquer on two wheels. In this episode of Bagerstop, you can hear Tobias Hansen’s incredible story: from cardiac arrest to mountain summit. Support the show

item.title: I don’t understand the connection between prostate and Chevrolet
item.description: Our correspondent Mads is in Italy for this week’s episode of Vuelta a España, which doesn’t make much sense. The conversation ranges from Kim A’s drifting, sports nougat, the Vuelta team time trial, Seven Coaching, and Niklas Landin, and ends with friend of the podcast “Larsen” joining in for a chat about prostate and Chevrolet. All that and much more in this episode of Lang Distance.

item.title: 138. Ed Clancy: The truth behind breaking my shoulders for aero gains, and other stories
item.description: This week, James and Will are joined by three-time Olympic gold medallist Ed Clancy. At 16, Ed was scouted by British Cycling’s Academy, where he came up alongside riders like Mark Cavendish and Geraint Thomas. By 2008, at age 23, he had won his first Olympic gold in the Team Pursuit. Two more golds followed in the same event at London 2012 and Rio 2016, along with a bronze in the Omnium in 2012, making Ed one of the most successful British cyclists of all time and one of only a handful of athletes to win gold at three consecutive Olympics. Over a career spanning two decades, Ed won dozens of track medals at World and National level, broke multiple world track records, and had an illustrious road career with one of Britain’s most successful domestic teams, Rapha-Condor, before retiring in 2021. Today, Ed works as the active travel commissioner for South Yorkshire, advises British Cycling on various projects, mentors and coaches, and has just written a new book, Full Gas Forever, a guide for cyclists over 40 who want to ride faster and further, co-written with Lexie Williamson. In this episode, Ed talks about life under Dave Brailsford, people joking about breaking his shoulders for aerodynamic gains, turning 40 and how that changed his cycling mindset, accidentally “winning” the Fred Whitton sportive, and what racing was like in his day compared with what riders face now. Ed’s new book, Full Gas Forever, is published by Bloomsbury and available here. Interview begins at 12.20. This episode is brought to you by the Hammerhead Karoo GPS bike computer. item.title: 137. The Strange Tale of Charly Gaul, with Paul Maunder
item.description: This week James and Will (well, mostly James) are joined by author Paul Maunder, who discusses his book Angel of the Mountains: The Strange Tale of Charly Gaul. Born in Luxembourg in 1932, Charly Gaul turned professional at 20 and quickly established himself as a prodigious but mercurial talent. “A murderous climber with a lower gear than the rest, turning his legs at a speed that would break your heart — tick tock, tick tock, tick tock,” said fellow rider Raphaël Géminiani. In 1956 Gaul won the Giro d’Italia, followed by the Tour de France in 1958 and the Giro again the following year. Yet despite such success, he was a fragile and curious figure, prone to mistakes, distrust, and neurosis, and by 1965 he had retired, never again reaching those heights. But that is only half the story, as Angel of the Mountains follows Gaul through a strange and tragic retirement into a more peaceful old age. Angel of the Mountains: The Strange Tale of Charly Gaul is published by Quercus and available in all good bookshops and online. item.title: 136. Kim Le Court, Monument Winner, Stage Hunter, and History Maker
item.description: This week Robyn and Will are joined by Kim Le Court. The 29-year-old Mauritian turned pro in 2015 and now rides for Women’s WorldTeam AG Insurance-Soudal. In that time, she has taken a Giro d’Italia stage win, six national titles, and earlier this year won a thrilling Liège–Bastogne–Liège Femmes. In this episode, Kim talks about having to petition teams to sign her despite her obvious talent; making history as the first African to win a Monument; coping with the mental strain of racing and the comedown after winning; her emotional ride to her first Grand Tour stage win at the 2024 Giro; and embracing the role of domestique while still chasing big victories, including her hopes for a Tour de France Femmes stage win this summer. item.title: 135. Stephen Roche: the Triple Crown Champion
item.description: This week James and Will are joined by Stephen Roche, who in 1987 became only the second rider in history to win the Giro d'Italia, Tour de France, and World Championships in one season, the Triple Crown (pub fact: the other was Eddy Merckx in 1972, and Tadej Pogačar joined them in 2024). In this episode, Stephen talks us through that incredible season, from the rollercoaster Giro that left him fearing for his safety, to crossing the line first in Austria to cap a historic year. Stephen also shares stories from his early years at the notorious French amateur club ACBB; falling out with team bosses and sponsors; proving the doubters wrong; crashes and injuries; and what he makes of the sport today. This July, Stephen will ride three stages of the Tour de France as part of the Tour 21 venture, raising money for Cure Leukaemia along the way, a charity close to Stephen's heart after his son Florian was diagnosed with the disease at just seven years old. Find out more about Stephen's efforts and how you can support him or get involved at thetour21.co.uk ------------------ This episode is brought to you by Dagsmejan sleepwear, the innovative sleepwear that boosts recovery, aids sleep and helps power pro team Tudor Pro Cycling.
